raw_text: ACHESON, A. B.—B. 1885; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1913); on mil. service, 1914-1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920.
ACLAND, FREDERICK A.—B. 1861; journalist, England, U.S.A., and Canada; apptd. sec. dept. of lab., Canada, 1907; deputy min. of lab., 1908.

ACTON, ROGER DAVID.—Cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1896; collr. of land rev., Perak, June, 1900; astt. sec. to res., Perak, Jan., 1903; dep. regisr., sup. ct., Penang, May, 1907; ag. sheriff and deputy regisr., Penang, Dec., 1907; deputy regisr. and astt. offi. assignee, Apr., 1908; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, Dec., 1910; promoted to Cls. III., Apr., 1911; 2nd mag., Singapore, Apr., 1911.
ADAMS, C. H.—Astt. dist. commr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; dist. commr., Mar., 1917.

ADAMS, ERNEST VICTOR.—B. 1875; ed. at Cranleigh Sch., Surrey and City of London Coll.; served artifices, Quan. Survey, London, 1891-1894; passed prelim. exam. Surv. Inst., 1895; Fellow of the Registd. Corp. Acctsts. (F.B.C.A.), 1915; tech. astt., accts. br., H.M. Office of Works, London, 1894-1895; acctnt. clk., civ. engrnr.-in-chf's dept., Admiralty, in charge, corresp. br., ag. on various occasions as ch. survr., 1895-1900; transf. to Simon's Bay Dkdy. Extension, Cape Colony, as senr. acctnt. clk., in charge accts. and corresp., S.C.E.'s Office, 1900-1906; served as astt. in Simon's Bay Dkdy. Vol. Rifles (Boer War), 1901; recd. comm. in Cape Car. Artillery, Sept., 1902, prom. captain, Mar., 1903; ch. acctnt., Engrnr., India, 1906-1910; astt. sec., Engrnr., England, 1910-1912; acctnt. and cnhr., Gold Coast Harbrs., Accra and Secondee, 1912-1918; rly. acctnt., in charge rly. and pub. wks. accts., Anglo-French occuptn., Togoland, 1918-1919; astt. rec-gen., Gambia, 22nd May, 1919.
ADAMS, LOUIS, I.S.O. (1912).—B. 1853; ed. at Felsted Schl.; entd. crown agents' off., 6th Apr., 1871; ch. cashier, 1st Feb., 1883.

ADAMS, PERCY TARGETT.—B. 1863; ed. at Epsom Medical Coll., 1874 to 1879, Guy's Hosp. and Durham Univ., 1881 to 1886; res. med. offr., Kent County Ophthalmic Hosp. (9 years), and a further 3 years as hon. full surgeon; for 10 years astt. M.O.H. of Borough of Maidstone and deputy M.O.H. and astt. pub. analyst for County of Kent; med. offr., G.P.O., London, 1897; astt. M.O.H., Port of Bombay, 1898-1900; retd. to med. dept., G.P.O., London, 1900; govt. analyst and bacteriologist, O.R.C., June, 1903; and astt. M.O.H. for Union of S. Africa at Bloemfontein.

ADAMS, THOMAS, F.S.I.—B. 1871; ed. Corstorphine and Edinburgh; pres., Young Scots Soc., 1900; sec., Garden City Assoc., 1901-1904; sec. and man., First Garden City Co., 1903-1906; town planning adviser to Marquis of Salisbury, Earl of Lytton, Sir Richard Paget, Bt., and other landowners, 1906-1909; town planning inspr., L.G.B. of England and Wales, 1909-1914; examr. in civic design, Univ. of Liverpool, 1912-1914; rep. of L.G.B. on arterial roads; Confes. of Greater London, 1913-1914; prep'd. offl. repts. on housing and town planning investigtns. in U.S., Germany, Sweden, and other countries and on first town planning schemes in Britain, 1908-1918; organised cheap cottage exhibits., Letchworth, 1905, and Wolverhampton, 1908; housing and town planning adviser to Canadian Govt. since 1914; hon. mem. Instn. of Mun. and Cnty. Engrns. (England); mem. of American Soc. of Mun. Improvements; pres. Town Planning Inst. of Britain, 1913-1914; and mem. of coun., 1914-1919; mem. bd. of govt., American City Planning Inst., 1915-1918; chmn., Town Planning Inst. of Canada, 1919.

ADAMSON, JOHN ERNEST.—B. 1867; ed. in London; M.A., London Univ.; principal, Normal Coll., Pretoria, 1902; dir. of educn., Transvaal, 1906; M.L.C., Transvaal; mem. of coun. of Cape University; dir. of educn., Transvaal Prov.
ADAMSON, HON. W. A.—M.L.C., Victoria; somcnr. of pub. wks., 1915-1917.

ADDISON, JOSEPH BARTLETT, M.B.E. (1918), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Ed. at St. Mary's Hosp., London; astt. med. offr., Seychelles, 1907; chief med. offr., 1908; ag. priv. sec. and clk. to exec. coun. from May to Nov., 1912.
ADELAIDE, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. ARTHUR NUTTER, THOMAS, D.D., Cantab.—B. 1869; rector of Guisborough, Yorks., 1901-1906; bishop of Adelaide since 1906.

ADERS, WALTER MANSFIELD.—B. 1881; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Univ. Coll., London; Marburg Univ., Prussia, Ph.D. (science honours) 1902; engaged on research work, Marine Biological Assoc., Plymouth, 1903; astt. to pathologist, Egyptian govt. hosp., Cairo, and curator of pathological museum, 1905; res. offr. of rinderpest serum station, Abassia, Cairo, 1906; priv. astt. and scientific sec. to dir. of quarantine bd., Alexandria, 1907; detailed to El Tor for research work re cause of dysentery amongst pilgrims; research work with Prof. Viguier Algiers (marine biology); research work, Welcome Laboratory, Khartoum, 1909; London Schl. of Trop. Med., 1910; research work, Zanzibar, since 1911; recd. Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 2nd cls., for services rendered, 1912; economic biologist, 1st June, 1917; author of numerous papers and reports on medical subjects.

AGBEBI, DENYI.—Astt. warder, Lagos prison, 1888; 4th clk., treas., 1888; examg. offr., customs, 3rd Aug., 1888; paymr., Ibadan detchmt., Haute Force, 15th May, 1897; in conjunction, postmr. and clk. to req., Ibadan, 14th Dec., 1901, to Mar., 1903; audit clk., June, 1903.

AGBEBI, EPHEMIA MICHAEL EKUNDAYO.—B. 1884; B.A., Durham Univ., 1903; ed. at Educational Inst., Sierra Leone, and Fourah Bay Coll. (Arabic prizeman); 2nd on list, S. Leone civ. ser. exam., Nov., 1899; tutor, S. Leone gram. schl., 1904; audit clk., Lagos, 1905; lecturer at teachers' evening classes for govt. certifs., 1906-7; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, 1908.

AIKINS, SIR JAMES A. M., KNT. BACH. (1914), K.C.—B. 1861; ed. Upper Canada Coll., Univ. of Toronto; graduated in arts; called to the bar, Ontario, 1878; Manitoba, 1879; Q.C., 1884; counsel for the C.P.R. Co. at Winnipeg, 1881-1911; pres. Canadian Bar Assoc., 1914-1916; lieut.-gov., Manitoba, 1917.

AINSWORTH, JOHN, C.M.G. (1900), D.S.O. (1918), C.B.E. (1919).—Impl. Brit. E. Africa Coy., 1889-1896; sub-comsnr., E. Africa Prot., 1896; ag. dep. comsnr., E.A.F., 1904-1909; in charge of Ukamba Prov., 1895-1906; Naivasha Prov., 1906-1907; Nyanza Prov., 1907-1917; pres. of special comsnr., Tanalang, 1909; mil. comsnr. for labour, E. Africa Exped. Force, 1917-1918, with local rank of colonel; chief native comsnr., E.A.F., 1918; mem. exec. and legis. couns., R.A.P.

AINSWORTH, THOMAS WILLIAM.—B. 1891; ed. Leeds Gram. Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1913; M.A., 1918; cadet, Hong Kong civ. ser., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, 1917; ag. sec., sanitary bd., 1917; seconded for mil. serv., 1917; ag. dep. regir. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1919; ag. 2nd astt. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1919.

AKITOVE, DANIEL.—B. 1866; ed. at Lagos Gram. Schl.; copyist to Queen's Advocate, Lagos, Aug., 1886; was fourteen years regir. of instruments affecting land; dep. regir. of births, deaths, marriages, and aliens, Jan., 1896; ag. regir. at intervals; clk. to commr. of lands (now dir. of surveys), Apr., 1901.
ALBURY, G. A.—B. 1865; entd. Bahamas civ. serv., 1898; Out Island comsnr. (1st div.), 1909; sisal inspr., pro tem., Jan., 1919; also ag. S. and C. mag. from Aug., 1919.
ALBURY, JOSEPH JOHNSON.—B. 1881; Out Island comsnr. (4th div.), Bahamas, 1910; 3rd div. comsnr., Jan., 1918.
ALBURY, STANLEY VICTOR STRATHMORE.—B. 1873; tidewater, Bahamas, 1901; clk., post office, 1907; Out Island comsnr. (3rd div.), 1911; (2nd div.), 1912; supt. of hospital, 1917.
ALBURY, WILTON GRANT.—B. 1877; public school teacher, Bahamas, 1896; resigned, 1906; re-apptd., 1909; head-master, boys' central school, 1914; inspr. of schools, 1916.

ALDWORTH, JOHN RICHARD OLIVER.—B. 1866; ed. at Cheltenham College; second asst. collector and magistrate, Klang, 23rd March, 1889; passed in Malay, 11th July, 1889; asst. coll. and mag., Rawang, 11th July, 1889; first asst. coll. and mag., Klang, 1st December, 1889; asst. dist. officer, Kuala Selangor, 23rd December, 1889; ag. dist. officer, Kuala Selangor, 14th August, 1890; ag. treasurer of Pahang, 12th July to 12th November, 1892; dist. officer, Klang, 3rd July, 1893; dist. officer, Ulu Langat, 1st December, 1895; ag. collector of land revenue, Seremban, 17th Sept., 1896, to 30th Sept., 1897; examiner in Malay, Negri Sembilan, 1896; ag. collector of land revenue, Kuala Lumpur, and registrar of titles, Selangor, 15th February, 1898; collector of land revenue, Seremban, and registrar of titles, Negri Sembilan, 15th June, 1900; sec. to High Commissioner, Fed. Malay States, 25th Sept., 1901; ag. sec. to res.-gen., F.M.S., Sept., 1901, to Feb., 1902; ag. sec. to res. of Selangor, Feb., 1902, to Apl., 1904; dist. off., Kuala Kangsar, Aug., 1903; ditto. K. Lipis, Feb., 1906; ag. sen. warden of mines, F.M.S., Dec., 1905, to Nov., 1906; inspr. of trade and cust., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1907; title altered to comsnr. of trade and cust., 1st Jan., 1908; ag. audr.-gen., Oct., 1909; ag. fed. sec., Dec., 1910; under-sec. to gov., F.M.S., Mar., 1911; protector of labour, F.M.S., Sept., 1911; title altered to controller of labour, Jan., 1912; ag. British res., Negri Sembilan, May, 1914 to Apl., 1915; cont. of labour, F.M.S., Apl., 1915; also ag. Br. res., Selangor, June to Dec., 1917; ag. British res., Negri Sembilan, July, 1919.

ALEXANDER, EDWARD BRUCK.—B. 1872; ed. Forest Schl., and Trin. Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1895; offl. asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Aug., 1897; pol. mag., Pandure, Aug., 1899; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Dec., 1900; Kegalle, Oct., 1903; seconded addl. supt. of pol., Cent. Prov., May, 1905; supt. of pol., S. Prov., July, 1906; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol., Mar.-Nov., 1907, and Dec., 1908 to Jan., 1909; supt. of pol., Cent. Prov., Nov., 1907; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, June, 1910; ag. govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Aug., 1911; chmn., mun. coun., and mayor of Colombo, Apl., 1913; on military duty Sept., 1914; govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Apl., 1919.

ALEXANDER, GILCHRIST GIBB.—Ed. at Glasgow Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; M.A., with 1st cls. hon. in mental philosophy, 1893; Eglinton Fellowship; gold medal as most distinguished graduate in arts of year; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1896; chief pol. mag., Fiji, Mar., 1907; ag. atty.-gen., and mem. exec. and legis. couns., Fiji, June, 1907, to May, 1908, June to Aug., 1908, Aug., 1910, to Feb., 1911, and from Oct., 1912, to Sept., 1913; seconded for serv. with W. Pacific High Comsnr. on special mission to Peru, Oct., 1908, to Apl., 1909, and to Tonga, July-Aug., 1911; ag. British judge of joint court, New Hebrides Condominium, Feb. to Oct., 1912; chmn. of comsn. to enquire into shipping conditions of colony, 1913-1914; ag. chief just., Fiji, and chief jud. comsnr., W. Pacific, Sept., 1913 to Jan., 1915; on mil. service, July, 1917; special comsnr. to deal with land disputes in Brit. Solomon Is., 17th Oct., 1919.
ALEXANDER, J. E.—Aast. land ranger, E.A.P., Oct., 1912.

ALEXANDER, THOMAS.—B. 1851; sub-inspr. Jamaica constab., Jan., 1872; 3rd class inspr., Oct., 1873; 2nd class inspr., Nov., 1876; 1st cl. inspr., 1877; 1st cl. certif. Hythe schl. of musk., 1879; course of prison training, Wormwood Scrubbs, 1884; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons, Jamaica, Apr. to Oct., 1904, and Nov., 1904, to May, 1905.

ALLARD, HON. JULES.—B. 1869; ed. Nicolet Coll., Quebec; lawyer; elec. to legis. assem., Quebec, 1897, 1900, 1904; apptd. to legis. coun. and min. of pub. wks., Quebec, 1905; min. of agric., 1907; min. of lands and forests, 1909-1917; re-elec. to legis. assem. at bye-election, 1910 and g.e., 1912; re-called to legis. coun., 1916.

ALLARDYCE, KENNETH JAMES.—B. 1881; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schl. and Wellington Coll., Berks.; jnr. clk., col. sec.'s off., Fiji, Apl., 1898; cadet, Apl., 1901; asst. comsnr., Colo West, Sept., 1901; ch. clk. and interp., native office, Jan., 1903; native lands comsnr., Apl., 1906; stip. mag., Nov., 1907; 1st grade mag., June, 1910; comsnr., Colo North, June, 1910; stip. mag., Nadroga and Colo West, and comsnr., Colo West, Nov., 1910; dist. comsnr., Levuka, and comsnr., Lomiviti, Jan., 1914; ag. native comsnr., Jan., 1914; M.L.C., Apl., 1914; native comsnr., May, 1914; sec. for native affairs, Jan., 1916; on mil. service, May, 1917.

ALLARDYCE, SIR W. L., K.C.M.G. (1916), C.M.G. (1902). Knt. of Grace of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem (1916).—B. 1861; clk. and interp., Provincial Dept., Fiji, 1879; transf. to Rotumah, 1882; stip. mag., July, 1882; asst. native comsnr. and stip. mag. and inspr. of native taxes, Kadavu, Jan., 1890; mem. exec. coun., Nov., 1893; native lands comsnr. and mem. legis. coun., 1894; native comsnr., 1895; asst. col. sec. and receiv.-gen., Dec., 1898; deputy gov., 14-20 Feb., 1901; administrator, govt., 18th July, 1901, to 9th Sept., 1902; col. sec. and receiv.-gen., 18th July, 1902; gov., Falkland Is., 1904; gov., Bahamas, 28th Dec., 1914; assumed govt. 15th June, 1915; gov., Tasmania, 1920; editor of native newspaper "Na Mata," 1890-99; author of a digest of Native Regulations, 1877-1900; attended first Fed. Conf. on Education, 1907.

ALLDER, WM. HOWELL.—B. 1873; 5th clk., C.S.O., Barbados, 1892; 4th do., 1898; 3rd do., 1900; 2nd cls. clk., P.O., 1904; ch. clk., do., 1908; ch. clk., audit off., 1913; acted on several occasions as clk. of the exec. coun. and comte., as acctn. in P.O., and as auditor-gen.; sec. to Barbados quarantine comsn., 1903, to West Indian quarantine comsn., 1904, and to comsn. on trade relations between West Indies and Canada, 1910.

ALLEN, F.T.—Enlisted as trooper in Rhodesian field force, 1900, and served in S. African war; invalided home 1901; obtained comsn. as 2nd lieut. in 5th Batt. Royal Irish Rifles, Dec., 1901, and returned to S. Africa until end of war; passed Hythe musketry and machine gun course, 1903; military course of instruction, 1906; captain, May, 1906; dist. supt. of police, Uganda Prot., July, 1907; cadet course, Royal Irish Constabulary, 1907; supt. of police, Uganda, 1911.

ALLEN, GEORGE JAMES, M.B.E. (1920).—B. 1879; entered C.O. Oct., 1896; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. ser., and reassigned to C.O., Oct., 1898; staff clk. in accounts dept., 11th Oct., 1910; seconded for serv. as acctnt. in the min. of mun. of war, Aug., 1915; dep. to astt. dir. of munitions finance, June, 1916; chief astt., secretariat (estabmt.), Dec., 1917; deputy astt. sec., Feb., 1919; regional finance offr., min. of health, 1st Mar., 1920.

ALLEN, GEORGE THOMAS, C.M.G. (1913), I.S.O. (1903).—Acctnt. to treasry., Victoria, 1895; sec. to treasry., Commonw. of Australia, Jan., 1901; comntr. under Old Age Pensions Act, Mar., 1909; attended Impl. Confec., 1911; ret.
ALLEN, H.—Tempy. astt. dist. comntr., R.A.P., Apl., 1919.

ALLEN, H. T.—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O. Mar., 1898; conf. clk., Dec., 1907; also clk. to concessions and finance comtes., Sept., 1910 to June, 1916; promoted, under order-in-council of 1910, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 16th June, 1916; astt. sec. to trade relations comtee., Aug., 1917; sec., Dependencies of Falkland Islands comtee., Jan., 1918; sec., raw materials board, Dec., 1918; astt. priv. sec. to sec. of state (Lord Milner), 1st Jan., 1920.

ALLEN, HON. SIR JAMES K.C.B. (1917), M.A., Camb.—B. 1865; entd. New Zealand Parlmt., 1887; lieut.-col. in command Otago Division, New Zealand Garrison Artillery, 1902; min. of defence since 1912; min. of finance and min. of educn., 1912-15; min. of finance and min. of external affairs, 1919.
ALLEN, LEONARD GILBERT.—B. 1901; entered C.O., 1917; apptd., after exam., astt. clk., 23rd Apl., 1919.

ALLEN, RAYMOND CECIL, C.M.G. (1917), F.R.G.S., A.M.I.C.E., F.S.I.—B. 1872; ed. at Roy. Univ. of Ireland and Queen’s Coll., Cork; dipl. in engrnr., Queen’s Coll.; astt. engrnr. water supply and main drainage, London City Coun., 1st Aug., 1895; resig. 1st Dec., 1900; chief survr., Uganda Prot., 23rd Nov., 1900; land officer, prim. regiar. of documents, and ag. comntr. of mines, 1st Apl., 1905; in charge of Crown forests, 1st May, 1905, to 31st July, 1907; regiar. of titles, 12th June, 1908.
ALLEN, W. C.—Astt. dist. comntr., E.A.P., Apl., 1914.
ALLPORT, GEORGE.—B. 1863; barrister and solicitor; ed. Bishop’s Schl., New Zealand; entd. N.Z. marine dept., 1875; clk., 1878; sec. and regiar. of seamen, 1903.

ALLWOOD, JAMES, C.M.G. (1903).—2nd clk., gov.’s sec.‘s off., Jamaica, July, 1862; ag. 2nd clk., exec. comtee. off., Feb., 1865 to May, 1866; clk., immigrtn. dept., May to Oct., 1866; 1st class clk., finance off., Oct., 1866 to Dec., 1869; supervisor of dist. post offices, Oct., 1871, to Mar., 1876; 1st class clk., col. sec.‘s off., Nov., 1886, afterwards astt. col. sec. to 1897; collr.-gen., 1877-1904; ret. 1904.
ALTONA, FREDERIK ADOLF BERTHOLD.—Ck., off. of the Mast. of the High Court, O.R.C., Sept., 1902; examnr. of accts., July, 1906; clk., O.F.S. provl. admnstr., 29th Aug., 1913.

AMERY, LIEUT.-COL. L.C.M.S.—B. 1873; ed. at Harrow and Balliol Coll., Oxford; late Fellow of All Souls, Oxford; Ouseley Scholar of Imp. Inst. in Turkish; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1902; on editorial staff of The Times, 1899-1909; served during the war in Flanders, Salonika, and the Near East; on the staff of the Impl. War Coun. at Versailles, and as astt. sec. to the War Cabinet and Impl. Cabinet to Jan., 1919; under-sec. of state for the colonies, 14th Jan., 1919; acted as sec. of state during Lord Milner’s absence in Egypt, Nov., 1919, to Mar., 1920; chmn., overseas settm. comtee.; capt. 14th lst B’ham Batt., Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1914; lieut.-col. on general staff, 1917; M.P. (U) for S. Birmingham, May, 1911; ditto Sparkbrook div., Dec., 1918; edited “The Times History of the South African War”; author of “Problem of the Army,” 1903; “Fundamental Fallacies of Free Trade,” 1906; “The Great Question” (with J. M. Robertson, M.P.), 1909; “Union and Strength,” 1912.

AMIRAYAN, G. G.—Called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1894; clk. and translator, comntr.’s office, Larnaca, Cyprus, Oct., 1878; translator, high ct. of justice and ct. of Temiyiz, 1881; regiar., sup. ct., 1883; was sec. to comntr. apptd. in 1888 to inquire into organzat. of Cyprus pol.; was examnr. in Turkish, 1886; sec. to legal bd., and examnr., 1901; astt. King’s Advocate, 16th Apl., 1906; wrote Turkish translation of draft penal code for Cyprus, 1897, and English translation of Ottoman commercial code, 1906.
AMORY, HOWARD WHITEHOUSE.—B. 1855; extra rev. offr., St. Kitts, 1893; rev. offr., quarantine offr., and clk., bd. of health, Nevis, 1895; cashier, treasry., St. Kitts, 1897.

AMPHTHILL, 2nd BARON, creat. 1881, OLIVER ARTHUR VILLIERS RUSSELL.—Son of the 1st Baron; born 1869; succeeded his father, 1884; G.C.S.I. (1904), G.C.I.E., 1900. Ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxon. Rowed in the Oxford eight, 1889-91; pres. of the Oxford Union, 1891; B.A., 1891; M.A., 1900; astt. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Sec. of State for Cola., June, 1896; priv. sec., June, 1897; one of the British delegates to the Internat. Sugar Conference, Brussels, May and June, 1898; lieut. Roy. 1st Devon Yeomanry Cavalry; capt. 3rd batt. Bedfordshire Regt.; J.P.; and prov. grand master of Freemasons, Bedfordshire; governor of Madras, Oct., 1900-1905; ag. Viceroy of India in 1904.

ANDERSON, CHARLES OSBORNE.—B. 1868; 2nd clk. and letter carrier, P.O., Bahamas, 1888; chief clk., 1890; ag. rec. just., Inagua, 1896; ag. warehouse keeper and examnr. offr., 1906; ag. postmtr., 1907, 1909, 1910; ag. auditor of pub. accts., 1911; postmtr., 1913; mem. H. of A.
ANDERSON, E. L. B.—Astt. dist. comntr., E.A.P., Oct., 1919.

ANDERSON, GEORGE BARTLEY.—B. 1880; ed. Gordon’s Coll., Aberdeen; clk., acct.-genl.’s dept., G.P.O., London, 1896; clk., war office, Jan., 1900; 2nd cls. clk., G.P.O., Johannesburg, Dec., 1902; transf. to exec. and legis. councils dept.; 2nd cls. clk., June, 1903; 1st cls. clk., July, 1905; acct.s clk. and cashier, Transvaal agt.-general’s office, London, Oct., 1907; astt. acctnt., Apl., 1908; clk., P.W.D., Nyasaland, June, 1909; chief clk., secretariat, Apl., 1914; ag. astt. chief sec., Aug., 1916; on re-organisation of secretariat apptd. 2nd astt. sec., Aug., 1919; ag. 1st astt. sec., Aug., 1919.

ANDERSON, MAXWELL HENDRY.—C.B.E. (mill.) (1919); O.B.E. (1918); K.C., Aug., 1919; capt., R.N. (ret.); entered Royal Navy, 1893; ret. at own request, 1912; recd. thanks of Admy. for valuable series of magnetic observations carried out in the Pacific Ocean; instructor, R.N. Coll., Portsmouth, 1908-10; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1909; younger brother of Trinity House, 1910; rejoined Navy on outbreak of war; employed on special services; reed. thanks of Admy. for services rendered to naval prize fund and at courts martial; specially promoted capt., Apr., 1919; govt. mem., central (unemployed) body for London, 1913 to 1919; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, 9th May, 1919; ag. chief just., 5th July to 22nd Aug., 1919.

ANDERSON, MAY CHRISTINA, R.R.C. (1900).—Probationer at col. hosp., Fiji, 1894-7; certif., 1897, and apptd. staff nurse; sister-in-charge, 1899; matron, 1902; also visiting matron to govt. lunatic asylum, 1903; mem. of Royal Brit. Nurses' Assoc., and mem. Australian Trained Nurses' Assoc.

ANDERSON, ROBERT MOWBRAY.—Entd. govt. printing dept., St. Vincent, 6th Feb., 1886; chief govt. printer, July, 1894; steward, col. hosp., Aug., 1901; mem. of comte., to inquire into admn. of poor relief, Aug., 1904; clk. to regt., sup. ct., and regt. of B., M. and D. for dist. I., Nov., 1904; tempy. ast. landing waiter for spec. excise duties, Nov., 1904; edited first "Illustrated Handbook of St. Vincent," 1907.
ANDERSON, R. T. H.—Asst. supt. of police, E.A.P., 1911.

ANDERSON, THOMAS EDWARD.—B. 1877; excise offr., B. Honduras, Dec., 1902; 2nd grade customs and excise offr., July, 1905; ag. 1st grade customs and excise offr., from April, 1904; confirmed March, 1906, rlyw. acctnt., 1st Aug., 1909; on completion of rlyw. construction reverted to substantive appt., 7th Sept., 1910; ag. chief clk., etc., treasy. and customs dept., 20th May to 7th Sept., 1911.
ANDERSON, T. J.—Chief, entomological div., agric. dept., E.A.P., May, 1908.
ANDERSON, Dr. W. J., M.A., LL.D.—B. 1854; Graduated Queen's Univ., Ireland; instr. of schls., New Zealand, 1884-1905; asst. inspr.-gen. of schls., 1906-14; dir. of educn., 1915.

ANDERSON, WILLIAM PATRICK, C.M.G. (1913), C.E.—B. 1851; ed. Bishop's Coll., Lennoxville, Quebec, and Manitou Coll.; asst. engnr., dept. of marine, Canada, 1874; chief engnr. since 1882; mem. of lighthouse and geographic boards; mem., pub. serv. comte., national serv. board, 1916.
ANDERSON, WM. ROSS, I.S.O. (1919).—B. 1865; pol. mag. and warden at Castlemaine, Victoria, Nov., 1893; sec. for mines and water supply, Nov., 1902; sec. to law dept., Victoria, Aug., 1906.

ANDRADE, LOUIS ANTONIO, C.M.G., 1918.—B. 1865; veterinary offr., Zanzibar, 1893; master of the horse, 1896; collr. of inland rev., 1st Jan., 1904; town collr., 1st Apl., 1907; collr. (now dist. comsrr.), 1st Jan., 1910; 2nd cls., Brilliant Star, Zanzibar; 2nd cls., Alih; 3rd cls., Hamoudieh; comdr. of Mil. Order of Christ, Portugal; chevalier of the Order of the Immaculate Conception, Portugal.

ANDREW, ROBERT CHARLES.—Ed. Liverpool Inst., Harper-Adams Agric. Coll., Newport, and Liverpool Univ.; B.Sc. (agric., London); National Diploma in agric.; mem. (by exam.) of Surv.'s Institn.; lecturer under the East Sussex county council, 1905 to 1910; foreign experience, 1911; asst. supt. of agric., N. Nigeria, 24th April, 1912.
ANDREWS, HENRY LEONARD GREYSTONE.—B. 1887; sub-inspr., Trinidad constab., 12th Mar., 1913.

ANGLIN, Hon. FRANCIS ALEXANDER.—B. 1865; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Montreal, and Univ. of Ottawa; called to the bar, 1888; K.C. 1902; apptd. sen. puisne judge, exchequer div., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1904; puisne judge, supreme ct. of Canada, 1909.

ANNETTS, HERBERT HENRY.—B. 1888; Ed. Magdalen Coll., Oxford, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1910; asst. master, preparatory schl., Repton, 1910-1911; served in Oxfordshire educn. comtees. office, and inspr. of evening schls., Oxfordshire county council, 1911-1912; apptd. schoolmaster, educn. dept., Northern Nigeria, 12th Sept., 1912.

ANSON, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR ARCHIBALD EDWARD HARBOURD, R.A., C.M.G. (1876), K.C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1826; entered the royal mil. acad., Woolwich, 1841; presented with sword for exemplary conduct; 2nd lieut., R.A., June, 1844; 1st lieut., 1st Apr., 1846; 2nd capt., 9th July, 1852; 1st capt., 1st Sept., 1856; brevet major, 21st July, 1864; lieut.-col., 12th May, 1866; brevet col., 12th May, 1874; col., 23rd Dec., 1875; ret. with the hon. rank of maj.-gen., 26th August, 1879; served at the siege of Sebastopol in 1855; Crimean medal and clasp, 5th class Medjidie, and Turkish medal; inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius, Sept., 1858; was employed in 1862 as the responsible mem. of the Br. mission to the court of Radama II. of Madagascar, on the occasion of his coronation; lieut.-gov. of Prince of Wales Is., Feb., 1867; admsd. govt. Str. Sttlmts. 1871, 1877, and 1879; conducted the successful military operations for repelling the invasion of the state of Sungei Ujong, 1875-76 (medal and clasp); ret. 1882; J.P. for Sussex.
ANSTEY, EDWARD A.—Comsrr. of crown lands and min. of agric. and repatriation, S. Australia, 1918.

ANTHONISZ, JAMES OLIVER, C.M.G. (1914).—Cadet, S. Sttlmsts., Oct., 1883; B.A., St. John's Coll., Camb. (Senior Optime); asst. Indian immigr. agt., Singapore, May, 1886; 3rd mag., Singapore, 1888; 2nd ditto, July, 1892; ag. official assignee and regir. of deeds, Singapore, Oct., 1894; and Apr., 1897; ag. 1st mag., July, 1895; offl. assignee and regir. of deeds, May, 1897; prn. munic. comsrr., Sing., Jan., 1901; comsrr., court of requests, Sing., 1st Jan., 1902; 1st magis., Sing., Aug., 1902, but continued to act as pres. of the munic. comsrr.; seconded for 1 year as pres., munic. comsrr., 1st Jan., 1903; ag. treas., Sept., 1904; treas., May, 1908; pres. of coman. of inquiry into municipal affairs, July, 1909; ag. res. councillor, Penang, Apr., 1910; ag. Brit. rea. Selangor, Jan. to Aug., 1911; ret., 1914; temporarily served in C.O. from Nov., 1915 to Jan., 1917.

ANTHONISZ, RICH. GERALD, I.S.O. (1919).—B. 1852; served in educn. dept. and regir.-genl.'s dept., Ceylon; asst. regir.-genl., 23rd Feb., 1892; seconded for serv. as examiner of Dutch records, 16th July, 1899; archivist and librarian, 1st Jan., 1902.

ANTHONY, GEORGE WILFRED.—B. 1860; cadet, lands titles office, S. Australia, 1876; journal and index clk., 1884; searcher of titles, 1895; first dep. regir.-gen. of deeds, 1904; regir.-gen. of deeds and regir. of building societies, 1914.

ANTHONY, PHILIP ARNOLD, C.M.G. (1918), M.I.C.E.—B. 1873; ed. at Mill Hill Schl.; served in engineering dept., G.W. Bly., 1894-1910; gen. man. and chief engnr., F.M.S. Rly., 1910; services lent to ministry of munitions, May, 1915 to Nov., 1916, resumed duty in F.M.S., Feb., 1917; services lent temporarily to govt. of South Aust., Apr., 1918; resumed duty in F.M.S., Jan., 1919.
ANTROBUS, EDWARD GREGAN, C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1860, ed. at Charterhouse; entered Crown Agents' office, 31st Jan., 1879; asst. acctnt., 17th Jan., 1893; ch. clk., and acctnt., 1st July, 1902.

ANTROBUS, SIR REGINALD L., K.C.M.G. (1911), C.B. (1889).—B. 1853; ed. at Winchester and at New Coll., Oxford; scholar of New Coll., 1872; 1st class in classical mode, 1874; 2nd class in final classical school, 1876; B.A., 1876; apptd., after an open compet. exam., to be a clk. in the C.O., May 3rd, 1877; asst. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, 5th Dec., 1880, to Dec., 1882; priv. sec. to the Earl of Derby, 16th Dec., 1882, to 24th June, 1885; to Col. the Right Hon. F. A. Stanley, M.P. (afterwards Earl of Derby), 24th June, 1885, to 6th Feb., 1886; and to Earl Granville, 6th Feb. to 3rd Aug., 1886; adminstd. the govt. of St. Helena, 5th Nov., 1889, to 8th June, 1890; priv. sec. to Mr. Meade, 1st Feb., 1892, and to Mr. S. Buxton, M.P., 17th Aug., 1892; sen. clk., Jan., 1894; princ. clk., 1896; asst. under-sec. of state, Oct., 1898; crown agent for the cols., 1909; retired, 1918.

APPLEGATE, F. W.—B. 1887; ed. Bath Coll. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1911; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1915; junr. asst. col. sec., 13th May to 30th Sept., 1915; travelling commr., Nsawam, 1st Oct., 1915 to 31st May, 1916, and from 12th Apr., 1917; ag. dist. commr., Kwahu, 1919.

ARCHER, GEOFFREY FRANCIS, C.M.G. (1913).—Asst. collr., East Africa Prot., 1st Dec., 1902; attached secretariat, June, 1902 to Mar., 1903; dist. commr., 1st Apr., 1907; spec. serv., Northern Frontier, East Africa, 1909; offr.-in-charge, May to Dec., 1911; awarded Cuthbert Peat Grant by the R.G.S., 1918, for surveys in E. Africa connecting Major Gwynn's Abyssinian triangulation with the triangulation of E. Africa; transf'd. to Somaliland Prot., June, 1912; admstd. govt., July to Oct., 1912, and from June to Oct., 1913; dep. commr., 31st Mar., 1913; commr. (now gov.) and commr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., 15th May, 1914; assumed govt., 22nd May, 1914; present at operations against the Dervishes at Shimber Berri, Feb., 1915 (African General Service Medal and clasp); member of British Mission on occasion of coronation of the Empress Zauditu of Abyssinia, Feb., 1917 (Star of Ethiopia, 1st class).

ARCHIBALD, EDGAR S., B.A., B.S.A.—B. 1885; reed. pmrv. educn. at Yarmouth pub. schl. and Yarmouth acad.; grad. Acadia Univ., 1903; also grad. from the Nova Scotia hort. schl. in the same year, and from Nova Scotia agric. coll. in 1906; reed. deg. of Bach. of Scien. Agric. from the Ontario agric. coll., 1908; inst. of agric. and expmtist. at the Nova Scotia agric. coll., 1908; prof. of agric. and farm supt. of the same coll. in 1910; entd. the serv. of the Fed. Dept. of Agric. in June, 1912, as Dominion animal husbandman at the Cent. Exptnl. Farm, Ottawa; ag. dir. of Exptnl. Farms, 1918-1919; dir., June, 1919.
ARCHIBALD, GEORGE.—B. 1869; entd. Trinidad civ. ser., Jan., 1892; cashier, Trinidad govt. rly., 1894; acctnt., 1906.

ARCHIBALD, MUNGO TENNENT, M.C.—B. 1885; ed. Glasgow (M.A.), and Pemb. Coll., Oxford; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; offr. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., May, 1910; ditto, Batticaloa, May, 1911; pol. mag., Kurunegala, May, 1912; asst. land settm't. offr., Jan., 1913; special comsrs. in connection with riots, June, 1915; asst. land settm't. offr., Aug., 1915; on military duty, 1916; asst. land settm't. offr., Aug., 1919.

ARMBRISTER, PERCY W. D.—B. 1862; ag. regis., prothonotary, and clk. of Crown, Bahamas, 1884; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s dept., 1886; clk., pol. ct., Nassau, 1887; J.P., 1890; res. mag. and collr. of rev. Abaco, 1890, Eleuthera, 1894, Harbour Is., 1895, Inagua, 1896; ag. stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas, and judge of ct. of com. pleas, 1896 and 1897; regis. of cta., prothonotary, and clk. of Crown, Jan., 1897; Out Island comsrs., 1909; recvr.-gen. and treas. 1916; ag. col. sec., 19th Sept. to 12th Nov., 1919.

ARMBURSTER, HUBERT, M.B.E. (1919).—B.A., ed. at Caius Coll., Camb.; asst. collr., B.C.A. Prot., Aug., 1899; ag. dist. mag., Marimba dist., Apr., 1905; dist. res., 2nd cls., Apr., 1906; res., 1st grade, Oct., 1915.

ARMITAGE, CAPT. CECIL HAMILTON, C.M.G. (1911) D.S.O. (1901).—Asst. inspr., G. Coast constab., 1894; served in Ashanti exped., 1896-96 (star); Northern Territories (Neutral Zone), 1897 (medal and clasp); inspr., 1898; priv. sec. to gov. of G. Coast and clk. of leg. and exec. couns., 1899-1900; Ashanti campaign, 1900 (ag. res. during siege of Coomassic, D.S.O., medal and clasp); trav. comsrs., 1901; comsrs. of S. Province of Ashanti, July, 1901; comdt. of Ashanti Mines volunteers with rank of major, Nov., 1905; ag. chief comsrs. of Ashanti, Oct., 1901, to end of year, Apr. to Oct., 1906, Dec., 1907, June, 1908, and from June to Dec., 1909; chief comsrs. of Northern Territories, 28th Jan., 1910.

ARMISTEAD, CHARLES ALFRED—3rd treasy. asst., East Africa Prot., 19th Apr., 1900; 2nd treasy. asst., 13th Nov., 1901; paymr., 3rd K.A.R., 1st Apr., 1904; dep. treas., Nyasaland Prot., 3rd Oct., 1918; ag. treas., 11th Nov., 1919.
ARMSTRONG, C. B.—Man., Naiyasha stock farm, E. Africa Prot., May, 1919.

ARMSTRONG, HON. JAMES WILLIAM, B.A., M.D.—B. 1860; ed. Pictou Academy, Acadia Coll. (B.A.), Manitoba Med. Coll. (M.D.), and King's Coll. Hospital, London; physician; pres. and dir., telephone and elec. light co., Gladstone, Manitoba; health offr. for Gladstone dist.; mem., bd. of health, Manitoba, 1897-99; elec. to legis. assem., Manitoba, gen. elec., 1897; re-elec. each subsequent elec.; prov. sec. and municipal comsrs. in Norris admtn., May, 1915.

ARMSTRONG, ROBERT.—B. 1879; ed. 'Friends' Schl., Great Ayton, Yorks and Durham Univ.; diploma in agric., Armstrong Coll., Newcastle-upon-Tyne, in Univ. of Durham; lecturer, schl. of agric., Egyptian govt., 1906-1910; lecturer and organiser in agric. for the Hampshire County Council; asst. dir. of agric., Zanzibar, 6th Feb., 1913; ag. dir. of agric., 28th Oct., 1915 to 13th Aug., 1916; ag. chief supply offr., 16th Sept. to 15th Nov., 1918, and 30th Mar., 1919; ag. admrn.-gen., 10th Nov., 1919.
ARNOLD, DR. FRANK ARTHUR—Dist. M.O.H., Eastern Transvaal, 1902; asst. M.O.H., Transvaal, 1903; dist. M.O.H., Northern Transvaal, 1906; M.O.H., Transvaal, 1910; M.O.H., Union of S. Africa, 1911.

ARNOLD, G. EMERSON, M.D., B.S., Durham, M.R.C.P., London, M.R.C.S., England, J.P.—B. 1872; dist. med. offr., Fiji, 6th May, 1901; ag. senr. med. offr., 1912-13; dist. med. offr., and M.O.H., Lautoka, 4th Apr. 1913.

ARNOLD, W. J. J.—Royal Univ., Ireland; B.A. (hons.), 1887, M.B., 1894; D.P.H., Oxon., 1913; civ. surgeon attached to R.A.M.C., St. Helena and S. Africa, 1900 to 1903; apptd. col. surgeon, St. Helena, Apr., 1903; M.E.C., 1911; ag. gov., Sept., 1911 to Feb., 1912.

ARTHUR, JAMES STATIN WILLS.—B. 1881; ed. at Marlborough Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1904); cadet, S. Stintins, Jan., 1906; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, Sept., 1907; ag. asst. treas., Singapore, June, 1909.

ARUNACHALAM, SIR PONNAMBALAM, KT. BACH. (1914).—B. 1853; ed. Colombo Academy and Christ's Coll., Camb.; Ceylon govt. scholar at Camb.; foundation scholar at Christ's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1875; M.A., 1879; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1875; apptd. after compet. exam. to Ceylon civ. ser., Apr., 1875; filled various judicial offices as pol. mag.; comsnr. of requests and dist. judge from 1875 to 1904; ag. regiar. gen. of lands and of B. M. and D. in the Island, and fiscal of W. Prov., Mar., 1887; re-organized the depts., for which received thanks of govt.; confirmed as regiar. gen., Jan., 1898; supt. of decennial census, Mar., 1900, to June, 1903; received Diamond Jubilee Gold Medal, 1897; offr. of cls. I. grade L. of the civ. ser., Jan., 1908; offr. M.L.C. from 1906; mem. of exec. coun., 16th Jan., 1912; has served on numerous comsrs. (higher educa., tuberculosis, registn. of titles, notaries, etc.); pres. of Royal Asiatic Soc. (Ceylon branch), of the Ceylon Univ. Assoc., and of Ceylon Social Service League; vice-pres. of Ceylon Agric. Soc.; has written largely on Ceylon history, antiquities, vital statistics, and Indian religions and philosophies; is engaged on a codification of the Civil Law of Ceylon (vol. I., published in 1910); ret., 1913.

ASHBURNHAM, JOHN ANCHITEL.—B. 1865; clk. to admstr., Brit. Bechuanaland, Oct., 1885; sec. to Bech. admstr., Feb., 1888; accompanied high comsnr. to conference with pres., S.A.R., at Blignaut's Pont, Mar., 1890, and at Colesberg, Apr., 1893; sec. to concessions comsnr. for Bech. Prot., May, 1893; C.C. and R.M. at Taunga, Brit. Bech., Feb., 1894; C.C. and R.M. at Gordonia, June, 1894; asst. comsnr. for Bech. Prot., Nov., 1895; R.M., Bloemfontein, Sept., 1901; chrmn., land laws inquiry comsnr., O.R.C., Oct., 1901; ag. col. sec., O.R.C., Apr., 1904, and Oct., 1906.
ASHFORD, HON. W. G.—M.L.A., N.S. Wales; min. of agric., 1914; sec. for lands and forests, Nov., 1916.

ASHLEY, Rt. Hon. ANTHONY EVELYN MELBOURNE, P.C. (1896).—Son of the 7th Earl of Shaftesbury; born 1836; ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.A., 1855; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1863; was for some time sec. to the late Lord Palmerston; is a mag. for Dorset, and was treas. of county cts. of Dorset from 1863 to 1874; is 2nd church estates comsnr.; parly. sec. to the Board of Trade, Apr., 1880; parly. under-sec. of state for the cols., 12th May, 1882, to 24th June, 1885; sat in the House of Commons for Poole from May, 1873, to Mar., 1880, and for the Isle of Wight from Apr., 1880, to Nov., 1885; is author of a Life of Lord Palmerston.

ASHTON, DANIEL ASPINALL.—B. 1869; ed. at Manchester gram. schl. and Owens Coll.; M.B., B.Ch., Victoria Univ.; med. offr. in charge of a branch, Cheadle asylum, Manchester; civ. surg., S. African war, 1901-1902 (medal and three clasps); col. med. offr., S. Nigeria, Jan., 1903; served in Cross River expdn., 1904 (medal and clasp); med. offr. at Abe and Obubura.

ASPINALL, ALGERNON EDWARD, C.M.G. (1918)—B. 1871; ed. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; hon. sec., West India Club, 1898-1902; sec. of West India Comtee. since 1898.

ASTWOOD, EDWARD WILLIAM.—Junn. clk., prisons dept., Jamaica, 1st Mar., 1875; 3rd cls. clk., treasy., 31st Dec., 1875; ag. 2nd cls. clk., treasy.; 1st cls. clk., treasy., 1887; ch. clk., and acctnt., treasy., 23rd Nov., 1909; ag. treas. and man. of govt. savings bank, 14th June to 13th Dec., 1915.

ATCHLEY, C., C.M.G. (1911), I.S.O. (1902).—B. 1850; entered the C.O., Aug., 1868; served in clk.'s, acctnt.'s, and gen. depts.; asst. clk., 2nd class, 1875; supt. of the library, 1st Oct., 1880; mem. libr. comtee., Imp. Instit., 1891; corresp. mem. of Instit. Colonial Universal, 1892; librarian, C.O., 1900; ret., 1915; edited "Lucas' Historical Geography of the British Colonies," vol. ii., West Indies, second edition, 1905; contributed numerous articles to 2nd Suppt. to Dictionary of National Biography.

ATHILL, SAMUEL LAUGHLAND, JUNR.—B. 1886; ag. clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Sept., 1901; ag. 2nd clk., regiar. off., Antigua, Nov., 1902, to Feb., 1903; ag. jnr. audit clk., Dec., 1903; acctnt., St. John's savings bank, Dec., 1903; ag. 2nd treasy. offr., Montserrat, May, 1906; clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Aug., 1906; sec. to legal affairs inquiry comsnr., Montserrat, 1911; ag. regiar., Montserrat circuit, Feb.-Mar., 1915; deputy coroner, dists. "A" and "B," Antigua, 6th Oct., 1917.

ATKINSON, THOS. HENRY.—Cadet, destitute poor dept., S. Aust., 1875; clk., 1878; 2nd visiting offr., 1884; 1st ditto, 1886; dep. supt. of dest. asylum, and visiting offr., 1889; supt. of dest. asylum, and sec. to bd., 1897; chrmn. of dest. bd., 1904.

ATTERBURY, JOHN LUCAS.—B. 1877; admitted a solicitor, June, 1899; served in S. African war, 10th Begt. Imperial Yeomanry (Royal Bucks Hussars), Jan., 1900 to June, 1901, medal and four clasps; dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 19th Dec., 1908; ag. prov. comsnr., Cent. Prov., May-July, 1911; ag. prov. comsnr., Western Prov., Nov.-Dec., 1912; ag. prov. comsnr., Cent. Prov., July, 1915, to May, 1916; ag. prov. comsnr., Western Prov., Nov., 1917, to May, 1918; ag. comsnr., Cent. Prov., Mar. to Dec., 1919.

AUBER, STEVEN JEREMIE.—B. 1858; temporary outdoor offr. customs, S. Leone, June, 1878; tide waiter, Aug., 1879; landing waiter, Sept., 1880; sen. ag. measuring survr. of shipping, Aug., 1882; clk. of customs, Gambia, 1883; ch. clk. and cashier, 1892; measuring survr. of shipping, Nov., 1902; clk. to navigation and pilotage bd., June, 1904; offr. in charge of cust. dept., 18th Sept. to 14th Nov., 1906.

AUCHINLECK, GILBERT GRAHAME.—B.Sc. (McGill), F.C.S., B. 1884; ed. West Indies and McGill Univ., Canada; 1st cls. hons. in chemistry and botany, McGill Univ.; gov't. laboratory, Leeward Is., 1902-04; McGill Univ., 1904-08; science and 2nd maat., St. Kitts-Nevis gram. schl., 1908-09; supt. of agric., Grenada, 1909-11; asst. dir. of agric. and chemist, Mauritius, 1914; ag. dir. of agric. and regiar., O.C. societies, Dec., 1916 to May, 1917; nominated M.L.C., Mauritius, Jan.-May, 1917; has served in defence forces of Antigua, St. Kitts and Mauritius; author of papers on land settlement and various tropical agric. subjects.

AUCHINLECK, WILLIAM DOUGLAS, I.S.O.—Appd. 3rd clk. treasy., Antigua, 10th June, 1867; 1st clk., 1873; landing survr., 1874; M.L.C., Nevis, 1877; treas., Nevis, 1879; J.P., 1879; visiting just. of prison, and mem. of hosp. bd., 1879; prot. of emigrts., 1880; M.L.A., Dominica, 1882; escheator-gen., Dominica, 1882; comanr. of wharfs, 1882; treas. and M.L.C., St. Kitta-Nevia, 1883; mem. of Leeward Is. federal coun., 1883; mem. exec. coun., St. Kitta-Nevia, 1886; chmn. hosp. bd. for many years; treas., Antigua, 1893; ag. col. sec., Leeward Is., 1895-96; J.P.; and visiting just. of prison, Antigua, 1897; M.L.C., Antigua, 1898; chmn. and comnrs. of taxes, Antigua, 1899; mem. exec. coun., Antigua, 1903; mem. of fed. exec. coun., 1903; chmn. quarantine bd., Antigua, 1906; chmn. of poor relief comtce., 1906; auditor general, Leeward Is., 1st Apr., 1907; comanr. to enquire into admnstr. of Cunningham hosp., St. Kitts, 1908; adminstr., Antigua, during absence of gov., 1908; has served on many comtces., and has held numerous acting appointments.

AUCKLAND, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. ALFRED WALTER AVERILL, D.D.—Ed. St. John's Coll., Oxford (2nd cls. Th. sch.), 1887; M.A., 1891; Ely Coll., 1888; deacon, 1888; priest, 1889, Lon.; formerly curate of St. George's, Hanover Square, 1898-91; Holy Trinity, Dalston, Middlesex, 1891-94; vicar of St. Michael's, Christchurch, N. Zealand, 1894-1909; chaplain of hosps., Christchurch Cathedral, 1902-1909; hon. canon of Christchurch Cathedral, 1902-1909; archdeacon of Akaroa, 1903-1909; Bishop of Waiapu, 1910-1913; Bishop of Auckland, 1913.

AUDEN, FRANCIS THOMAS.—B. 1869; ed. Shrewsbury; M.D., Edinburgh Univ., 1903; M.B. and C.M., 1891; formerly res. med. offr., Albany general hosp., Grahamstown, Cape Colony; dist. surg., Rustenburg, Transvaal; civil surg., S. African field force, 1900-1901 (medal and clasp); dist. comnrs. and govt. med. offr., Calcos Islands, Dec., 1914; govt. med. offr., Turks Is., Oct., 1916; mem. legis. bd., Sept., 1916.
AUDETTE, HON. LOUIS ARTHUR, B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1856; apptd. regis. of exchequer ct. of Canada, 1887; asst. judge, exchequer ct., 1912; puisne judge, 1919.

AUSTIN, JAMES.—B. 1867; apptd. to home civ. serv. in cust. dept., 1886; bd. of educn., 1891-1896; clk., educn. off., Natal, 1st Jan., 1897; 2nd cls. clk., audit dept., 1st Aug., 1900; re-transferred to educn. dept., 1st June, 1901; examin. offr. and statistical clk., 1st July, 1902; ag. acctnt. offr., 8th Aug. to 30th Sept., 1903; acctnt. and statistical offr., 1st Jan., 1904; ag. sec., 10th May to 9th Aug., 1907; prov. acctnt., Natal, 1913.
AUSTIN, ROBERT GORDON LEFROY.—B. 1871; ed. at Cheltenham Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford, B.A., 1894; M.A., 1899; Transvaal educn. dept., 1903; examntr., 1917.

AYLESWORTH, HON. SIR ALLEN BRISTOL, K.C.M.G. (1911), K.C., B.A. (1874), M.A. (1875).—B. 1854; educ. at Newburgh high school and Toronto univ.; Prince of Wales prizeman; bencher of law soc. of Upper Canada; called to bar, Q.C. (Ontario); one of H.M.'s comnrs. for settlmt. of Alaska boundy., 1903; elected to the H. of C. for North York, 1904; postmr.-gen. of Canada, 1905; min. of just., 1906; British ag. internat. fisheries arbitn. at the Hague, 1910; resigned portfolio and retired, 1911.
AYRE, A. D.—Chief acctnt., G.P.O., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1918.

AYTON, A. L.—B. 1878; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. ser. and assigned to science and art dept., Mar., 1899; to C.O. July, 1899; clk. in charge of acctns., Western Pacific high comm., Dec., 1913; ag. sec., W.P.H.C., 29th Mar., 1915; returned to C.O. as staff clk. in acctns. dept., 29th Jan., 1920.

BABER, EDWARD ALFRED.—B. 1888; copyist, survey dept., B. Honduras, 1906; apprentice, ditto, 1906; passed exam. in theory of land surveying; 2nd cls. clk., Aug., 1910; ag. 1st cls. clk., 1911; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Nov., 1913; clk., roads and rivers bd., 1914; ag. ch. clk. and clk. of coun., 1914-15; ag. inspr. of schls. and clk. to bd. of educn., Nov., 1915; senr. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Feb., 1917; ag. ch. clk., etc., Mar., 1917 to Apr., 1918, and from May, 1919.

BACKHOUSE, ALFRED PAXTON.—B. 1851; Crown prosecutor, N.W. dist., N.S. Wales, Oct., 1878; ditto, S. dist., Jan., 1881; dist. jud. dist., N.W. dist., 1884; judge, metropolitan dist., 1st Apr., 1892; Vice-Chancellor of Univ. of Sydney.
BACKLOG, THOMAS.—B. 1883; served in S. African war, 1901; in Gallipoli with Aust. Imp. Forces, 1914-16; inspr. of plantations, Zanzibar, 16th July, 1916.
BACKWELL, H. F.—B. 1884; ed. at Charterhouse (jun. and sen. scholarships), and at King's Coll., Camb., exhibr.; asst. mast., Portsmouth gram. schl., 1907-8; asst. rec., N. Nigeria, 19th Dec., 1908.

BADDELEY, FRANK MORRIS.—B.A., Magdalene Coll., Cambridge, 21st wrangler, Math. tripos, 1896; cadet, F.M.S., 1897; passed final exam. in cantonese, Dec., 1900; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1913; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, May, 1901; ditto, Serendah, Nov., 1901; recd. thanks of govt. in connection with Ulu Selangor riots, Feb., 1902; revenue auditor, Pahang, Jan., 1904; ag. dist. offr., Jelebu, Jan., 1905; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of chinese, Singapore, Aug., 1905; ag. dist. offr., Xmas Is., Oct., 1906; ag. offr. assignee, Singapore, Apr., 1907; apptd. a currency commr., May, 1907; dist. offr., Kuantan, June, 1908; aspt., govt. monopolies dept., S. Stltmsts., Sept., 1909; P.M.G., S. Stltmsts., Feb., 1916.
BAGGE, STEPHEN SALISBURY, C.M.G. (1907).—2nd cls. asst., Uganda Prot., 8th Oct., 1894; sub. comnrs., East Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1902; ret., 1910.

BAGSHAWE, ARTHUR WILLIAM GARRARD.—C.M.G. (1915), B.A., M.B., B.C., D.P.H., Camb., M.R.C.S., Eng., L.R.C.P., Lond.; ed. at Marlborough Coll. and Caius Coll., Camb., served in resident hosp. appts., 1898-1899; med. offr., Uganda Prot., Oct., 1900; served as med. offr. in Lango expdttn., 1901; served with Anglo-German bdry. comm., 1902-4; employed in sleeping sickness extended investigations in Uganda, Apr., 1906, to May, 1907; director of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908.
BAILEY, ALFRED LISLE.—B. 1885; extra clk., col. sec.'s off., Barbados, Mar., 1904; 4th clk. treasy., Mar., 1908; ag. 3rd clk. on several occasions.

BAILEY, EDWARD SHEPPARD.—B. 1887; 2nd clk. col. treas. Barbados, 1889; ch. clk. 1892; sec. to emigrn. comm., 1893; ag. col. treas., 1894 and on other occasions; clk. to plantation commrs., July, 1903; man. of agric. bank, 1907.
BAILEY, JOHN F.—Dir. of botanic gardens, Adelaide, S. Australia, 1917; formerly dir. of Brisbane gardens.

BAIN, Norman Kerr, B.A. Cantab.—Cadet, S. Stltms., Jan., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Sept., 1907; ag. astt. to res., Malacca, July, 1910; offr. of clv. V., 31st Mar., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, 29th Mar., 1914.
BAIRD, Matthew.—Min. of educn., Victoria, Nov., 1917.
BAKER, Alan Custance.—B.A., Oxon.; cadet, S. Stltms., Dec., 1908; attchd. to land office, Malacca, Jan., 1909.

BAKER, Clarence Frank Stewart, M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1873; ed. Wyggeston Sch., Leicester; articled pupil to City Engineer, Canterbury, 1899-1892; astt. engrnr., Canterbury, 1892-1894; astt., Rochdale sewerage scheme, 1894-1895; astt. borough engrnr., Stockton-on-Tees, 1896-1901; irrigation engrnr., Ceylon, 1901; divisional irrigation engrnr., 1911; ag. astt. dir. of irrigation, 1911; astt. dir. of irrigation, 1913; ag. dir. of irrigation, Nov., 1915 to Aug., 1916; dir. of irrigation, Jan., 1918.
BAKER, Clement John, L.R.C.P., M.R.C.S.—Ed. at Middx. Hosp.; S. African wrk, 1901-1902 (medal and two clasps); med. offr., Uganda Prot., 1st Jan., 1903.

BAKER, Ernest Laydlaw.—B. 1880; ed. Australia; clk., col. sec.'s office, Fiji, 1901; shorthand writer, legis. coun., 1906; sec., Albert Park Bd. (joint appnt.), 1908; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, and clk. to legis. coun., 1909; clk., exec. coun. (joint appnt.), 1910; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., 1913; dist. commr., Colo East, 1914; ditto, Ra., 1917; ditto, Savu Savu, 1918.

BALDWIN, Raymond de Courcy.—Ed. St. Peter's, York, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; astt. auditor, Sierra Leone, 3rd Apr., 1909; ag. auditor in Sierra Leone for periods in 1909, 1910, 1912 and 1913, and in the Gambia, July-Sept., 1911; astt. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1914; served with W.A.F.F., in German East Africa and Portuguese East Africa, June, 1917, to Nov., 1918; astt. sec. to adminstr., occupied territory, G.E.A., Nov., 1918; ag. sec. to adminstr., Aug. to Nov., 1919.

BALDWIN, William Frederick.—B. 1882; 2nd div. clk., War Office, July, 1901; army audit offr., Cape Town, 1904; army cashier, army acct., dept., O.F.S. and Cape Col., 1908; army cashier, Pretoria (attached to army pay dept.), for S. Africa, 1911; astt. auditor, col. audit dept., S. Nigeria, 2nd Aug., 1911; auditor, Seychelles, 12th Nov., 1912; ag. auditor, Nyassaland, Sept., 1914; attached for one month to audit offr., Mauritius, 1915; resumed post of auditor, Seychelles, Mar., 1915; transf'd to Brit. E. Africa for military service in connection with acct., Apr., 1916.
BALFE, Joseph Hamilton, M.D., F.R.C.S., Edin., J.P.—Dist. surg., Eshowe, Natal, 1890; medical supt., Durban hosp., 1898; dir., govt. cottage hosps., 1904; member Natal Medical Council.

BAFOUR, David.—Ed. Edinburgh Univ.; 1st prizeman in conveyancing, 1886; examr. in conveyancing, 1887; 2nd prizeman in polit. econ., 1890; admitted a solicir. in Scotland, 1888, and in Jamaica, 1898; olk. of courts, Jamaica, 1897; stip. mag., 1906; regiar. gen. of vital statistics, also regiar. of land titles, trade marks, etc., and dep. keeper of records, 1908; in charge of census of the colony, 1911; is a J.P. for Kingston; author of students' text-book on court of session practice, 1891.

BAFOUR, Douglas Hastings.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 16th Jan., 1911; attached to Jaffna Kachcheri, Feb., 1911; ag. pol. mag., Point Pedro, May, 1911; extra office astt. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., Nov., 1911; attached to Trincomalee Kachcheri, May, 1912; ag. office astt. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., July, 1912; pol. mag., Matara, July, 1913; astt. commr. of excise, June, 1917.
BALL, Hon. Richard Thomas.—M.L.A., New South Wales; sec. for pub. wks., and min. for Nya., Nov., 1916.
BALMER, A. B.—Asst. treas., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1918.
BALMER, F. E.—Dep. chief acctnt., G.P.O., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1919.
BAMBER, F. T.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Aug., 1914.

BAMFORD, Lieut.-Colonel H.W.M., O.B.E. (1918); M.C. (1916)—B. 1882; Cape Mounted Riflemen, 1900-1906; S. African War, 1900-1902; Transvaal Mounted Rifles, Zulu Rebellion, 1906; Natal Police, 1907-1912; dep. sheriff, N. Zululand, 1913-1914; adjt., Natal Light Horse, S.A. Rebellion and G.S.W., Africa, 1914-1915; adjt. 2nd S. African Infantry, Senussi Campaign, W.F.F. Egypt (wounded Hazalin Jan. 23rd, 1916); to France with S. Africa bde., 9th Scottish Div. (wounded Delville Wood, July 15th, 1916); bde.-major, 90th infy. bde., France, 1917; comdg. 2nd S. African infy., 1917-1918 (very severely wounded Beaurevoir, Oct. 8th, 1918); service in France includes Somme, Arras, Messines, Ypres, defence of Hazebrouck, Le Cateau; three times mentioned in despatches; O.B.E., M.C., Croix de Guerre, 1914-15 star, G.S.warmedal, Allies medal; commr. of police and inspr. of prisons, Malta, July 29th, 1919.

BANNERMAN, Charles Edward Woolhouse.—B. 1884; entd. civ. ser. secretariat, S. Nigeria (Calabar) govt., Aug., 1903; transf'd. to headqrs., col. secretariat, Lagos, during amalgamation, as 2nd ols. clk., 1906; 1st cls. clk., provincial secretariat, Calabar, 1908; acted as chief and conf. clk., 1909; resig. Aug., 1910; law student, Middle Temple, Nov., 1913; practised before the sup. ct., Gold Coast, as barrister, solr. and advoc., Jan., 1914, to 30th July, 1919; pol. mag. and coroner, sup. ct., G. Coast Colony, 1st Aug., 1919.

BARBADOS, Bishop of (founded 1824), Right Rev. Alfred Pakenham Berkeley.—Foundation scholar, Codrington Coll., Durham; B.A., 1883; M.A., 1908; D.D., 1917; consecrated Lord Bishop of Barbados, 1917; vicar of All Saint's, Antigua, 1886-88; vicar of the Holy Innocent's, Barbados, 1890-1900; rector of St. Phillip, 1901-06; dean and rector of St. Michael's Cathedral, 1906-17.

BARDO, Frederic Stanley Cook.—B. 1869; ed. City of London Schl. and Vickery's Naval Acad., Pts.; master mariner; astt. port offr., Zanzibar, 22nd June, 1904; port offr., 11th June, 1906; director of wireless telegraph, 1907 to 1914.
BARKER, R. D.—Tempy. astt. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

BARLEY, Jack Charles.—B. 1887; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford, B.A., 2nd ols. hona.; cadet, W. Pacific civ. ser., 15th Dec., 1911; attached to res. commr.'s offr., Apl., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Gizo, 1st Nov., 1912; ag. treasur. and collr. of customs, 16th Apl., 1913; passed cadet, 14th Apl., 1914; deputy commr., 29th July, 1914; dist. offr., Gizo, 1915; ag. res. commr., 19th Apl., 1917.

BARNARD, Sir Frank Stillman, K.C.M.G. (1918).—B.1866; entd. ser. of B.Columbia Express Co. in 1879; subsequently became gen. man. and pres. of the company; promoter, and dir. for many years of the B. Columbia Electric Rly.; identified with many other important interests; mem. of H. C., Canada, 1879-1896; alderman of Victoria, B.C., 1886 and 1887; mayor, 1895; ret. from active business life in 1906; lieut.-govr. of B. Columbia, Dec., 1914.
BARNES, A. C.—Computer, survey dept., E.A.P., Oct., 1914; ast. analyst, Apl., 1916.

BARNES, HAROLD CHARLES EDWARD, C.B.E. (1918).—Ed. at Smythe's Naval Acad., Portsmouth; Somerset House, 19th Nov., 1894; ast. aud., S. Leone and Gambia, 26th Jan., 1895, to May, 1897; ast. aud., E. Africa Prot. and Uganda Rly., 2nd May, 1897, to 13th Feb., 1899; local aud., Somaliland Prot., Feb. to Dec., 1899; ast. aud., Uganda Rly., 9th Dec., 1899; local aud., E. Africa Prot., 1st Nov., 1904.

BARNES, JOHN ALBERT, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1857; Examr. E. and A. dept., 4th Jan., 1875; sen. examr., 16th Aug., 1885; transf. to col. audit branch as olk. in charge of accts., 12th Aug., 1902; on amalgamation of col. audit branch with E. and A. dept. apptd. chief examr., 1st July, 1906; sen. olk., 1st Apl., 1908; ag. local auditor, E. Africa Prot., 27th Nov., 1908, to 3rd May, 1909; ast. dir. of col. audit, C.O., 1st May, 1910.

BARNES, JOHN FREDERICK EVELYN, C.M.G. (1901), M.I.C.E., M.I.C.E. Ire.—B. 1851; ast. county survr., Antrim, 1872; on Abercorn Estates, 1873-9; govt. survr., Natal, 1880; Durban boro. engrn. 1882; ast. col. engrn. and survr.-gen. Natal, 1888; ag. col. engrn., with seats in exec. and legis. couns. 1889-90; engrn., P.W. dept., 1894; ch. engrn. of P.W., 1897; hon. mem., Natal Inst. of Architects, 1902; comsnr. for Natal to St. Louis Exposition, 1904; ch. engrn., supernumerary staff, Natal militia, 1907; mem. govt. tender bd., 1907; retired.

BARNETT, ERNEST ARTHUR.—B. 1884; clk. and pay and qtrmr., 1900; sub-inspr., Fiji constab., 1906; attached to R.I.C. for training, 1908; inspr., Fiji constab., 1911; higher grade, 1914; ast. adjutant to local forces, 1914; capt., Fiji def. forces, 1914; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab. and sheriff, June to Dec., 1916; J.P. for the col., 1916; press censor, 1917; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab. and sheriff, 1917; chief inspr., 1918; ag. inspr.-gen. of prisons, 1918.

BARNETT, FREDERICK JOSHUA.—B. 1869; collr. of customs and inspr. of labour, British Solomon Is. Prot., 1906; organised customs and post office, 1906-1908; treas. and collr. of customs, Aug., 1912; is a dep. comsnr. for the Western Pacific; ag. res. comsnr., Mar. to May, 1907, June to July, 1908, Mar. to Nov., 1909, May to Aug., 1911, and from 22nd July, 1914.

BARRAUT, EDWARD HENRY.—B. 1868; ast. res. and mag., Sandakan, B.N. Borneo, 1890; mag., interior, 1896; sec. to gov., 1897; sessions judge, 1897; res., Province Alcock, 1898; res., East Coast, 1902; ag. judel. comsnr., 1904; res., interior, 1908; judge of high ct., 1909; res., West Coast, 1910; M.L.C., 1912.

BARRETT, EDWARD.—Magte.'s olk., Mount Ayliff, July, 1901; ast. magte., Nov., 1901; ag. chf. clk., native affairs dept., Cape Town, June, 1907; confirmed, 1908; ag. under-sec. for native affrs., Cape, 1910; under-sec. for native affrs., Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1911.
BARRETT, Lieut.-Col. W. E. H.—Subaltern, 3rd K.A.R., 5th Apr., 1902; ast. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 15th Feb., 1907; dist. comsnr., Aug., 1912; comdg. 5th K.A.R., 1916.

BARRON, MAJOR-GEN. SIR HARBY, K.C.M.G. (1909), C.V.O. (1907).—B. 1847; ed. at Tunbridge Wells, and Roy. Mil. Acad., Woolwich; cadet, Roy. Mil. Acad., 1865; lieut., R.A., 1867; capt., 1879; major, 1884; lieut.-col., 1894; col., 1898; major-gen., 1904; adjutant, 1st Forfar Artil. volrs., 1890-84; chief instructor, schl. of gunnery, Shoeburyness, 1897-1900; commdg. R.A., Malta, 1904-8; retired, 1908; gov. of Tasmania, 22nd July, 1909; gov. of W. Australia, 1912-1917.

BARRY, HON. JEREMIAH HAYES, K.C.—B. 1858; ed. at public schls. of Saint Mary's and Fredericton; mem. of Senate of Univ. of New Brunswick; admitted to the bar, 1882; K.C., 1898; judge, sup. ct., New Brunswick, 1909; King's Bench division, 1913.

BARTH, JACOB WILLIAM, C.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Wadham Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1898; M.A., 1898; barrister-at-law, Mid. Tem., 1900; regis. and prin. regis. of documents, E. Africa Prot., 8th Aug., 1902; Crown advoc., 1st Oct., 1902; apptd. to serve on land comsnr., 31st Oct., 1904; judge, High Court, E. Africa Prot., 1st April, 1905; atty.-gen., E.A.P., Feb., 1914; ag. chf. sec., Aug., 1918.

BARTLETT, C. A.—Ck., senr. police mag.'s court, Bridgetown, Barbados, Jan., 1881; ag. in rev. offr., May, 1883 to Nov., 1884; chief olk., pol. mag.'s courts, dist. "A," June, 1897; ag. pol. mag. on numerous occasions; pol. mag. and revr. of wrecks, dist. "F," Nov., 1911; mem. of police comsnr., Jan., 1913; pol. mag., coroner and revr. of wrecks, dist. "B," Apl., 1913; mem. of cane fires comsnr., Apl., 1914.

BARTLETT, FRANK.—B. 1872; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1895; ast. collr. of cust., Trincomalee, June, 1897; pol. mag. and ast. collr. of cust., Jaffna, Sept., 1898; dist. judge, Tangalla, Mar., 1899; off. ast. to govt. agt., Uva Prov., Jan., 1900; dist. judge, Badulla, July, 1900; pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1902; dist. judge, Negombo, Oct., 1903; ast. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Feb., 1905; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1910; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apl., 1910; govt. agt., N. C. Prov., June, 1911; govt. agt., Prov. of Uva May, 1912; ditt., May, 1913.

BARTLETT, HUMPHREY EDWARD GIBSON.—B. 1880; ed. Winchester and Trin. Coll., Oxford, 2nd cls. class. moda., 2nd cls. final honour, schl. of jurisprudence; B.A., 1903; M.A., 1906; solr., 2nd cls. final honours, 1906; ast. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 1909; dist. comsnr., 1913; ag. prov. comsnr., Dec., 1915 to June, 1916; and from 23rd June, 1919.

BARTLEY, Wm., M.B.E. (1919).—B.A., Dublin; cadet, S. Stltmts., Dec., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Labuan, Nov., 1909; ag. ast. to atty.-gen., S. Stltmts., Apl., 1910; passed in Malay, Jan., 1910; passed in law, July, 1910; offr. of cls. V., 1st Jan., 1912; ag. ast. dist. judge and 4th mag., Singapore, 2nd Jan., 1912; ag. inspr. of prisons, 30th Jan., 1912; passed in Javanese, 21st Feb., 1912; dist. offr., Dindings, 25th May, 1912; ag. ast. sup't, Netherlands Indian immgrts., F.M.S. and S. Stltmts., 11th June, 1912; ag. ast. controller of labour, F.M.S., 10th Dec., 1912; ag. ast. to atty.-gen., Singapore, 21st Oct., 1914 to 19th Mar., 1915; ast. registr. of imports and exports, S. Stltmts.
BARTON, C. J. J. T.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Aug., 1914.
BARTON, C. M.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Aug., 1913; res. mag., June, 1914.

BARTON, FRANCIS RICKMAN, C.M.G. (1905).—Formerly a capt. in W. India Regt.; priv. sec. to lieut.-govr. of B.N. Guinea, 1896; res. mag. of B.N.G., 1903; admstr. of govt. of B.N.G. (now Papua), 6th Apr., 1904; first minister at Zanzibar, 1906; retired, 1913.

BASTEDO, SAMUEL TOVEL.—B. 1865; ed. Oxford schools; priv. sec. to premiers of Ontario, 1880-1896; dep. commr. of fisheries for Ontario, 1898-1906; British representative on internat. fisheries comm. between U.S.A. and Canada, 1908; resigned apptmt. as fisheries commr. to accept position of supt. of Canadian govt. annuities, Sept., 1908.

BATEMAN, WALTER SLADE.—Served in convict and prisons branch, Cape Colony, 1894-1901; sec. bd. of management, Somerset hosp., Cape Town, 1897-1901; clk., prisons dept., Transvaal, Jan., 1901; chief clk., July, 1901; ag. inspr. of prisons, June, 1903; asst. dir. of prisons, July, 1906; ag. dir., Nov., 1906; mem. of tender bd. at various times during 1902-1905; ag. gov. of central prison and local gaol, Pretoria, June, 1907; gov. of Johannesburg gaol and Diepkloof prison, Dec., 1907; ag. asst. dir. of prisons, Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1911; dir. and under-sec. for justice, 1918.

BATHURST, WALTER DUNDAS.—B. 1859; ed. Lancing Coll.; agt. of Congo Free State, 1883-6; apptd. to col. audit branch exchequer and audit dept., Jan., 1889; local audr., Gibraltar, under C. and A. G., Jan., 1891; ch. examr., E. and A. dept., col. audit branch, Dec., 1906.

BATTERBEE, HARRY F., C.M.G. (1918); C.V.O. (1919).—B. 1880; ed. at Grammar Schl., Faversham, and at Oxford; classical scholar, Hertford Coll., 1899-1904; 1st cls. class. mods., 1901; 1st cls. math. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1904; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., exchequer and audit dept., Jan., 1906; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., May, 1906; ag. 1st cls. clk., 4th June, 1916; 1st cls. clk., 6th July, 1917; sec. to W. African lands comtce., 1912; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis (now Lord) Harcourt, 9th Dec., 1912; priv. sec. to Lord Islington, 12th Oct., 1914; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Steel-Maitland, 31st May, 1915; priv. sec. to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Dec., 1916 to 13th Jan., 1919.

BATTISCOMBE, EDWARD.—Ed. at Edinburgh Univ. (medal for forestry) and in Germany; asst. conserv. of forests, E. Africa Prot., 15th July, 1904; dep. conserv. of forests, 1st Apr., 1907; conserv. of forests, 1911; hon. assoc. of Royal Scottish Arboricultural Soc.
BATTLEY, H. J. L.—B. 1887; apptd. after exam., 3rd cls. messenger, C.O., 14th May, 1912.

BAUMGARTNER, HAROLD VANE.—B. 1883; ed. at Bedford Grammar Schl.; clk., land settlt. dept., O.R.C., 16th Oct., 1902; clk. and typist, audit dept., 2nd Dec., 1902; clk., audit dept., 1st July, 1904; examr. of accts., audit dept., 1st Jan., 1908; junr. asst. treasr., G. Coast, 1913.
BAYER, CHAS. ALBERT.—Asst. survr., P.W.D. (Waterworks branch), S. Aust., 1882; sanitary engnr., 1888; hyd. engnr., 1902.
BAYLEY, BENJAMIN HAMILTON.—B. 1874; ed. at Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; draughtsman to titles to land commrs., B. Guiana, Oct., 1890; gradual promotion to 1st cls. clk., govt. sec.'s office, Nov., 1911.

BAYLEY, SYDNEY HOWARD.—Entd. P.W.D., B. Guiana, June, 1888; transf. to pris. dept., 1889; sec. to inquiry into coolie riots at Skeldon, 1896; seconded to audit office, 1896; commr. of taxation, B. Guiana, Feb., 1896; J.P., Mar., 1903; supt. of govt. indust. schl., Onderneeming, Aug., 1906.

BAYLY, MAJOR GEORGE C.—(5th Batt. Rifle Brigade).—Passed school of instruction, Mar., 1879; 1st class extra certif., Hythe, 1886; instructor of musketry, 1886; asst. inspr. G.C.C., Dec., 1883; dist. commn., Lagos and Badagry, 1884; ditto, Cape Coast, 1885; subinspr., Br. Honduras constab., Dec., 1888; mag., Orange Walk, Nov., 1889, to Nov., 1890; side-le-camp to Sir A. Moloney, Sept., 1891, to Jan., 1897; inspr., 1893; ag. inspr. commandant, 1894; ch. of police and excise, Grenada, Feb., 1898; M.L.C., Mar., 1899; mem. of St. Vincent police comm., 1903, recd. thanks of S. of S.; local commt. mil. police, and asst. commr., Cyprus, Dec., 1903; commr. of Papho, Cyprus, 1909.

BAYNES, EDWARD WILLIAM, O.B.E. (20).—B. 1890; clk., G.P.O., Antigua, Jan., 1899; clk. to commr., Virgin Islds., Jan., 1901; ag. priv. sec. and clk. to admstr., Dominica, Dec., 1906, to May, 1908; ag. clk., gen. legis. coun., Leeward Is., 1908 session; priv. sec. and clk. to adminstr. Dominica, 10th Aug., 1909; transf. to Impl. civ. ser. and apptd. 2nd ol. clk., National Health Insurance Comn. (England), 13th June, 1912; 1st cls. clk., 13th May, 1913; priv. sec. to sec., ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917; sec., National Health Insurance Joint Comtce., Oct., 1919; priv. sec. to parly. sec., ministry of shipping, Apr., 1920.

BAYNES, T. E. P.—B. 1884; clerical asst., col. sec.'s off., Trinidad, 1903; 6th olk., C.S.O., 1903; 5th olk., C.S.O., 1906; 1st olk., post office, Antigua, 1907; ag. 2nd olk., C.S.O., 1907; ag. 3rd olk., C.S.O., 1908, 1909, 1910 and 1911; 3rd olk., C.S.O., 1911; ag. 2nd olk., C.S.O., 1911-12; ag. clk. to admstr., Dominica, 1912; 2nd olk., C.S.O., clk. of exec. and legis. couns., Antigua, and supt. of govt. printing office, 1912; clk. to admstr., and clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Dominica, 1919.
BEAMISH, F. C.—Asst. conservator of forests, E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1919.

BEARD, CHARLES HALMAN.—Called to the bar, Leeward Is., Mar., 1875; awarded equity prize by coun. of legal educn., Lond., 1881; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Trin., 1882; ag. puisne judge, Leeward Is., on various occasions, 1886-1895; ag. solr.-gen., on various occasions, 1887-1889; ag. atty.-gen. (9 times), 1888-1898; apptd. solr.-gen., 15th Oct., 1889; res. mag., St. Mary, Jamaica, 28th Jan., 1898; regsr. friendly societies, Antigua, Apl., 1889, to Oct., 1893; mem. leg. coun., Antigua, 1889, 1890, 1896, and 1897; J.P., Antigua, Sept., 1889; visiting justice of gaol, Antigua, Sept., 1889 to 1897; res. mag., St. Mary, Jamaica, 1896; puisne judge, Jamaica, 1908.

BEATTY, DAVID.—Cadet. S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1898; passed final exam. in Chinese, June, 1901; ag. 3rd magis., Sing., Nov., 1901; ag. dist. offr., Christmas Is., July, 1903; 3rd mag., Penang, Oct., 1903; but cont. to act as dist. offr.; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., May, 1904; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Sing., June, 1904; ag. sheriff and dep. regiar., Penang, Dec., 1905; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, May, 1907; ag. offl. assignee, S. Sttlmts., Apl., 1909.

BEATTY, KENNETH JAMES.—B. 1878; ed. Univ. High Schl. and Melbourne Univ.; qualified Victorian bar, 1900; Transvaal law certif. (including Roman Dutch law), 1904; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1908; served in S. African war as lieut., V.M.I., wounded in action (Queen's medal and 4 clasps); apptd. pub. prosecutor, Transvaal, Nov., 1902; addtnl. asst. res. mag., Mar., 1903; asst. res. mag., Dec., 1905; attached Natal Carbineers, Natal, rebellion, 1906; rec'd thanks of Natal govt., medal and clasp; pol. mag., master of sup. ct. and regar.-gen., Sierra Leone, Sept., 1908; has acted as ch. just., circuit judge and atty.-gen., Sierra Leone.

BEAUCHAMP, 7th Earl (creat. 1815), Visct. Elmley (1815), Baron Beauchamp (1806, U.K.); K.G.; K.C.M.G. (1899); William Lygon; B. 1872; succeeded his father, 1891.—Ed. Eton and Chr. Ch., Oxford; D.L. Worcestershire; Mayor of Worcester, 1896-8; mem. London schl. bd., 1897-9; gov. N.S.W., 1899 to 1900; capt. of hon. corps of Gentlemen-at-Arms, 1906; first commr. of works, 1911-14; lord-pres. of the coun., 1910-15.

BEAUFORT, Sir L. P., KT. Bach. (1919), M.A., B.C.L.—Ed. at Westminster and Oxford; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1879; mem. of London schl. bd., 1888; gov't. sec. and judicial commnr., Brit. North Borneo, 1889; gov't. and comdr.-in-chief, Labuan and North Borneo, 1895-1900; chief just., N.E. Rhodesia, 1901; ag. admnstr., N.E. Rhodesia.

BEAUMONT, Sir W. H., KT. Bach. (1910).—Ensign 75th (Stirlingshire) Regt., Aug., 1870; lieut. Oct., 1871; served on the "Langalabalele Expedition" in 1873; ret., Aug., 1875; priv. sec. to Colonel Milles (administering the govt. of Natal), May, 1873; to Sir Benjamin Pine (lieut.-gov'), and clk. to the exec. coun., July, 1873; ag. R.M., Umlazi Div., Aug., 1874; clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1875; ag. gov't. clk. and clk. to the exec. coun., Oct., 1875; R.M., Newcastle division, Feb., 1878; col. comdt. of Dist. No. 1, Natal, at the commencement of the Zulu War, Jan., 1879, during which time he raised levies called the "Newcastle Scouts"; R.M., Insanda, May, 1887; R.M., City div., Pietermaritzburg, 1896; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., for various periods, 1st Feb., 1895, to Oct., 1902; judge of special treason ct., Oct., 1901; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1st Nov., 1902; admnstr., 6th June to 1st Sept., 1907; ret., 1910.

BEAVEN, REGINALD ALBERT GARDENER.—B. 1880; Ed. Malvern; solr., 1904; dist. commnr., G. Coast, 1907; ag. prov. commnr., 1911-12 and 1916; ag. chf. regar. and sheriff, 1915; asst. controlling offr., enemy property, 1918.

BECK, SIR ADAM, KT. Bach.(1914).—B. 1857; ed. in Galt; elec. to legis. assem., Ontario, 1902, 1905, 1908 and 1911; min. without portfolio, 1905-1914; mayor of London, Ontario, 1912-3-4; chrmn. Hydro-Electric Coman. of Ontario; defeated at g. e., Oct., 1919.

BECK, ARTHUR ANDREW.—Ck., csmnr.'s off., Cape Colony, 1st Mar., 1893; rlyw. dept., 1st Sept., 1893; asst. book-keeper, col. sec.'s off., 6th Jan., 1894; asst. acctnt., 31st Mar., 1899; inspr. of books and stores, 1st July, 1899, to 28th Feb., 1901; acctnt., 1st Apr., 1902; acctng. offr., 1st Oct., 1903; ag. financial adviser to sup't. gen. of educn., 3rd May, 1906; acctnt. to admnstr. Province Cape of Good Hope, 31st May, 1910.

BECK, Hon. NICHOLAS DO BOIS DOMINIC, K.C., LL.B.—B. 1887; educ. pub. and priv., schls., Coll. Inst., Peterborough, Ontario; called to the bar, Ontario, 1879; Manitoba, 1883; N.W.T., 1889; K.C., Dominion of Canada, 1893; judge, sup. ct., Alberta, 1907.

BECKETT, HAROLD.—B. 1891; ed. at Monmouth Grammar Schl. and Oxford; exhibitioner at Wadham Coll., 1910-1914; also Symons exhibitioner, 1912-1914; 1st cls. Class. Mods., 1912; 3rd cls. Lit. Hum., 1914; B.A., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 30th Nov., 1914; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Milner, 14th Jan. to 16th Mar., 1919; ag. 1st cls. clk., 17th July, 1919; priv. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir G. Fiddes), 1st Jan., 1920.

BECKWITH, CHARLES WILLIAM.—Entd. H.M.'s Navy, 1895, as lieut. holding extra master's certif., B. of T.; served in H.M.S. "Royal Oak" during occupation of Crete, 1908-1909; S.E. America as senior lieut., H.M.S. "Basiliak," and N. American Station in H.M.S. "Pallas," 1904; navigating lieut., H.M.S. "Diadem," flagship of Sir G. U. Noel, K.C.M.G., K.C.B., China station; ag. harb. mast., Hongkong, Oct., 1906; reverted to Royal Navy, May, 1907; asst. harb. mast., Dec., 1907; ag. harb.-mast., marine mag., emigtn. and cust. offr., regar. of shipping, supt. of gunpowder depot, collr. of light dues and supt. of imports and exports, 11th Nov., to 3rd Dec., 1908, and from 4th Aug. to 14th Oct., 1909; asst. supt., fire brig. (addtnl. appt.) 1st Jan., 1909; asst., supt., water police (second addtnl. appt.), 15th May, 1909; seconded as supt. of imports and exports, 17th Sept., 1909, to 31st May, 1910; ag. harbmr. on several occasions; supt. of imports and exports, 1st June, 1919.

BEDDOE, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1850; entd. civ. ser., Canada, as a 2nd cls. clk. and asst. acctnt. of Dominion Lands, 1883; 1st cls. clk. and asst. acctnt. of dept. of the Interior, 1885; chief clk., acctnt., 1899; liquidator of the N.W.T., 1909; supt'dg. acctnt. of the dept. of the Interior, 1908.

BEDWELL, HORACE, C.M.G.(1913), B.A., Jesus Coll., Camb.—Apptd. asst. dist. commnr. Niger (now S. Nigeria) Protec., June, 1896; wounded while on duty Qua Ibo Riv., Sept., 1896; ag. dist. commnr. Old Calabar, Dec., 1896-June, 1897; apptd. dist. commnr. Warri dist., Jan., 1898; sent up Niger to take over Asaba dist. from Roy. Niger Co., Dec., 1899; dist. commnr. Asaba, Dec., 1899-Sept., 1900; ag. divnl. commnr. Cent. (Niger) div. S. Nigeria Protec., June, 1901, to Dec., 1901; transf'd. to secretariat as ch. asst. sec., Feb., 1902; ag. sec. to govt., Mar.-Sept., 1902; ag. divnl. commnr. and ag. sec. to the govt., 8th Mar., 1902, to 24th Sept., 1902, 17th Apr., 1903, to 5th Aug., 1904, and on various occasions from 3rd Apr., 1905, to Mar., 1906; dep. high commnr., Apr., 1906; prov. commnr., 1st May, 1906; retired, 1919.
BEECH, M. W. H.—Asst. dis. commnr., E.A.P., 6th Dec., 1907; dist. commnr., Apr., 1914.

BELCHER, CHARLES FREDERIC, M.B.E.(1919), M.A., LL.B.—B. 1876; ad. at Geelong Gram. Schl. and Trin. Coll., Melbourne (final scholarship in classics and comp. philology, Shakespeare scholarship); admitted to practice, Victoria, Oct., 1902; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, June, 1909 (cert. of honour); conveyancer, Uganda Prot., July, 1914; custodian of enemy property, Uganda, Apr., 1915; mag., Uganda, June, 1916.

BELFIELD, FREDERICK, M.A. (Oxon.).—Barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; ag. collr. and mag., Pekan, May, 1889; confirmed, July, 1891; mag., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1896; ag. collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1896; confirmed, May, 1899; collr. of land rev., Kinta, N.E., 1899; ag. legal adviser, F.M.S., Sept., 1901; ag. commnr. of lands and mines, Nov., 1902; dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, Jan., 1903; legal adviser, F.M.S., Feb., 1906; ag. judicial commnr., July, 1906; atty.-gen., S. Stiltnits., Jan., 1910; judicial commnr., F.M.S., 1910; legal adviser, F.M.S., 21st Feb., 1911.

BELFIELD, Sir Henry Conway, K.C.M.G. (1914), C.M.G. (1909).—Ed. Rugby and Oriel Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1877; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1880; went W. circuit; mag.; collr. of land rev., and inspr. of schls., Selangor, 1884; ch. mag. and comsnr. of lands, 1888; ag. Br. res., June and July, 1889; sen. mag., Perak, 1891; ag. sec. to govt., Perak, July, 1891, to Jan., 1893; comsnr. of lands and mines, F.M.S., July, 1896; ch. examnr. in Malay for F.M.S., 1896; ag. British res., Selangor, on various occasions from Mar., 1897, to Oct., 1901; British res., Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1901; British res., Selangor, Aug., 1902; spec. miss. to Borneo, 1905; ag. comsnr. of lands, and as British res., Negri Sembilan, in addition to his own duties as British res., Selangor, on different occasions in 1905 and 1906; ag. res. gen. in addition, May to July, 1908; British res., Perak, 1911; offr. in attendance on T.H. the Sultan of Perak and Kedah at the coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; spec. mission to G. Coast and Ashanti to report on land tenure, Jan., 1912; gov. and comdr-in-chief, E.A.F., 21st Aug., 1912; assumed govt., 3rd Oct., 1912; retired, 1917; author of "Handbook of the Federated Malay States."

BELL, Archibald Groom, C.M.G. (1914), M.I.C.E.—B. 1868; ed. Felstead and Uppingham; employed on surveys Jamaica rlyw. extensions, 1887-8, and by Jamaica rlyw. co. after sale of line, 1889-90; astt. to W. Shelford, Esq., M.I.C.E., Apr. to Oct., 1890; astt. to Messrs. Hawkshaw and Hayter, 1890-1891; astt. engrn. P.W.D., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1891; astt. col. civil engrn., Oct., 1900; col. civil engrn., Aug., 1901; apptd. official member ct. of policy, Feb., 1901; D.P.W., Trinidad, Nov., 1907, and ex officio M.L.C. and M.Ex.C., April, 1908.

BELL, Edward, M.B.E. (1918)—Served in R.I.C., Dec., 1885 to Aug., 1896; attached 2nd Batt., Durham Light Infantry, 1896; Hythe certif. of musketry and instr.'s certif., Maxim machine gun; drill instr. to local force, St. Kitts-Nevis, 12th Aug., 1896; ag. inspr., L. Islda. police and adjut., St. Kitts-Nevis defence force, 1898; inspr., L. Islda. police, Sept., 1898; and capt. and adjut. defence force; ag. inspr.-gen., L. Islda. police, and ag. inspecting offr. of defence forces of colony, Apr. to Nov., 1903; ag. inspr.-gen., L. Islda. police, 1st Apr., 1906, subsequently confirmed in commd. of force as chief inspr.; comdt. local forces, with local rank of lieut.-col.; extra A.D.C. to gov. Sir Bickham Scott, 1912; mem. exec. coun., Leeward Is., 1915; police medal, 1915; recd. thanks of H.M.'s Govt., for services rendered during riot and disturbances in Antigua in March, 1918; is a J.P.
BELL, F. W.—Dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Jan., 1919.

BELL, Hon. Sir Francis Henry Dillon, K.C.M.G. (1915), K.C.—B. 1851; B.A., Camb.; barrister, Middle Temple, 1874; entd. New Zealand parl., 1893; leader of legis. coun.; min. of internal affairs, 1912; min. of immigrtn. in National ministry, 1915; also leader of legis. coun.; atty.-gen., 1918; and min. of eductn., min. of public health and min. of immigr. 1919.

BELL, George Patrick Cecil.—B. 1882; ed. Bedford grammar and modern schl.; midshipman, R.N.R., 1898-1901; ag. sub-insp., Jamaica constab., Mar. to Nov., 1901; 3rd cls. clk., revenue dept., Jamaica, Dec., 1901, to Mar., 1902; astt. supt. of pol., B. Hond., Apr., 1902, to June, 1906; ag. dist. comsnr., Orange Walk and Stann Creek dists. for short period in 1906; astt. comsnr. of pol., G. Coast, 14th July, 1906.

BELL, Sir Henry Hesketh Joudou, K.C.M.G. (1908), C.M.G. (1903).—B. 1865; ed. in Paris; 3rd clk. gov.-in-chief's office, Barbados, May, 1882; transferred to treasy. dept., Grenada, 1883; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Jan., 1890; sen. astt. treas., G. Coast, Aug., 1891; ag. dist. comsnr. and sheriff, Accra, 1892; ag. col. treas., 1893; rec-gen., Bahamas, Dec., 1894; represented Harbour Isld. in House of Assem., 1895-6; ag. col. sec., July to Oct., 1897, Apr. to Nov., 1898; admnr. of Dominica, Aug., 1899; ag. gov., Leeward Is., Aug., 1904, and from June, 1905; comsnr. and comdr-in-chief, Uganda Prot., 31st Jan., 1906; gov., ditto, 18th Oct., 1907; gov., N. Nigeria, 30th Oct., 1909; gov. Leeward Is., 1912; gov., Mauritius, 7th Jan., 1916; assumed govt., 18th May, 1916; author of "Geography of the Gold Coast," "Obeah," etc.
BELL, Leslie Livingstone.—2nd clk., govt. sec.'s dept., Papua, 20th Jan., 1906; chief inspr., dept. of native affairs and control, 20th Feb., 1909.

BERKELEY, CAPT. HUBERT.—B. 1864; midshipman, R.N.R., 1881; to Perak, 1884; pol. 1886; supt. of Penghulus, 1889; ag. collr. and mag., Upper Perak, 1891; asst. mag., Gopeng, 1894; ag. dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, 1896; dist. mag., Upper Perak, 1899; dist. offr., Klang, 1903; ditto, U. Perak, Sept., 1904; also warden of mines and asst. commr. of police for Upper Perak; capt., Worcestershire regt., 29th Jan., 1915; resumed duty as dist. offr., Upper Perak, Nov., 1917.

BERKELEY, MAURICE JULIAN.—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn; entd. col. serv., 1883; ag. pol. mag., Tobago, 1882; ditto, St. Vincent, 1884; ditto, Barbados, 1885-1892; pol. mag. Dist. "D," Barbados, 1892; ditto, Bridgetown, 1899; ag. judge, ct. of appeal, 1900 and 1901; S.J.P., Port of Spain, Trinidad, 1902; ag. judge, sup. ct., 1906, 1907 and 1908; ag. mem. of educn. board, 1907 and 1908; puisne judge, B. Guiana, 1908; ag. chief just., July to Sept., 1910, Apr. to Sept., 1912, May to July, 1913, and May to Nov., 1914; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1912, to Feb., 1913.

BERNE, CAPT. JAMES LKO, O.B.E. 1918—B. 1885; ed. Dulwich Coll.; 2nd Lieut., Royal Irish Regt., 1904; capt., 1910; served with Sierra Leone Batt. W.A.F.F., 1907-1912; served with Franco-Liberian bdry. comm., 1908 (red. thanks of French govt.); served with Anglo-French bdry. comm., 1911; capt., reserve of officers; asst. dist. commr., Somaliland Prot., 13th Sept., 1912; dist. commr., 28th March, 1914; served with Admiralty war staff intell. div., Apr. to Aug., 1916; accompanied mission to Abyssinia on the occasion of coronation of Empress Landitu, 1917; 3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia, 1917.
