Leone, Jan., 1897, to Apr., 1901; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Leone Prot., May, 1901; dist. comsnr., Oct., 1903; dist. comsnr. of Sherbro dist., Aug., 1905, and in addition dist. comsnr. of N. Sherbro dist., Mar., 1906; ag. curator of intestate estates, Nov., 1900, to Apr., 1901; is J.P. for the colony.

PAINE, JOHN JAMES.—B. 1863; served with 19th (Princess of Wales's Own) Hussars in Egypt, and gained medal and 3 clasps and Khedive's star for Suakin and Nile expeditions, 1884 and 1885; apptd. coal porter, C.O., 1st Jan., 1893; placed on estab. as office porter, 15th Apr., 1904.
PALETHORPE, RONALD FLINT.—B. 1891; ed. Hereford Schl. and Keble Coll., Oxford; asst. mast., St. George's Schl., Windsor Castle, 1913; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 5th Aug., 1914.
PALLISER, CHARLES FREDERICK WRAY, C.M.G. (1916).—Entered civ. ser., New Zealand, 1874; sec. to high comsnr. for N.Z. in London, 1909; ag. high comsnr., May to Aug., 1912; ret., 1916.

PALMER, ALFRED FELIX.—Auditor's clk., Grenada, Sept., 1879; rec. offr., St. Lucia, 1882; ag. ch. rev. offr., 1882-83; ag. ch. clk., treasy., 1883 and 1884; sub-clrk. customs and inland rev., and clk., 2nd dist. cl., Oct., 1884; acted twice as mag., and as coroner, dep. coroner, and J.P., ag. ch. clk., govt. office, 1893.

PALMER, CHAS. STUART RUSSELL, M.I.C.E.—Ed. at Roorkee Civil Eng. Coll., India; ent. P.W.D., India, Oct., 1878, as asst. engr.; spec. duty in England, 1886 to 1887; asst. to ch. engr., and asst. sec. to ch. comsnr., cent. provs., up to 1889, when apptd. exec. engr. of Hoochangabad div., and in 1892 of Nagpur div.; ret. from Indian serv., May, 1894; asst. survr., W. Aust., Oct., 1894; engr. in charge, roads and bridges, and harbours and rivers branches, Aug., 1897, of water supply, 1898, and sewerage, 1900; engr.-in-chief, June, 1902; insptg. engr. for W.A. in Lond., 1904.

PALMER, HAROLD.—B. 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser.; apptd. to national health insurance audit dept., 13th Jan., 1913; on military service, 2nd May, 1918, to 26th Feb., 1920; assigned to C.O., 8th Mar., 1920.

PALMER, HAROLD THORNBURY, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. (Liverpool); Guy's Hospital; asst. med. supt., Indo-European telegraph dept., Persia, and residency surg., Mar., 1903; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, 18th Aug., 1906; tempy. capt., Cameroons expeditionary force, Sep., 1915.
PAMELLONE, ARMAND JOSEPH, M.B., Ch.B. Edin.—B. 1879; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, 1st Feb., 1906; dist. med. offr., 1st Aug., 1913.
PANTIN, CHARLES GEORGE.—B. 1855; entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, 1st Jan., 1877; asst. aud.-gen., 1st Apr., 1912.
PARDOE, AVERN.—B. 1845; ed. Stratford-upon-Avon Collegiate gram. schl.; on staff of Toronto Globe, 1875-89; managing editor, 1883-9; librarian of Ontario legislature, 1898.

PARKER, JAMES T.—Cert. teacher, Leeward Is.; dist. postmr., Bethesda, 1901; schmtr., gov't. boys' reformatory schl., 1903; clk. and storekeeper, P.W.D., and clk. to road bd., Antigua, 1906; 1st govt. offr., Virgin Is., 1915; inspr. of weights and measures, Virgin Is., 1917.

PARKER, HON. SIR STEPHEN HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1914), KT. BACH. (1908).—Called to the bar, 1868; Q.C., 1890; mem. of legislature of W. Australia from 1878 to 1897; col. sec., 1892 to 1894; represented W. A. at the C.O., and gave evidence before sel. comtee. of H. of C. relative to the grant of self-govt. to the Colony, 1890; represented W. A. in London relative to the federation of Australia, 1900; justice of supreme ct., 1901; ch. just. of W. A., 1906-1913.

PARKER, WILLIAM ALSTEIN.—B. 1865; ed. Queen's coll., B. Guiana; entd. B. Guiana govt. serv., 1883; qualified as notary public and apptd., 1892; admitted solr., 1897; chief clk., and acct., admnstr.-genl.'s office, 1897; off. recvr. and pub. trustee, 1905; comsnr. of affidavits; has acted at various times as crown solr., stip. mag., admnstr.-gen., and regsr. of sup. ct.; served on coman. of enquiry into working of regsr.'s office, 1905.

PARKINSON, A. C. C., M.A. (1910).—B. 1884; ed. at Epsom and Oxford; class. demy, Magdalen, 1903-7; 1st cls. mods., 1905; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1907; apptd. after compet. exam., 1st cls. clk., acct., gen.'s dept., Admiralty, Feb., 1908; transf. as 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 19th Apr., 1909; sec., man. comtee. of imperial bureau of entomology; sec. to comtee. apptd. to investigate relationship between wild animals and trypanosomiasis, 1913-1914; sec. trop. diseases research fund advisory comtee.; sec. managing comtee. of trop. diseases bureut; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 5th Oct., 1914, to Mr. Bonar Law, 27th May, 1915, to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Dec., 1916; 1st cls. clk., 8th Dec., 1917; on military service from 16th May, 1915.

PARKINSON, J. W. H.—B. 1877; ed. Brighton Coll.; solicitor, sup. ct.; regsr. of high ct.; principal regsr. of documents; regsr. of companies; sec. for inventions and designs, E.A.F., 26th Mar., 1907; admnstr.-gen. and offr. recvr., Prov. Seyidie, E.A.F., 26th Aug., 1909; currency comsnr., E.A.F., 1909; sec., Waik. coman., E.A.F., 1914; custodian of enemy property, 1915.
PARNALL, J.—Solicitor; asst. aud., E. Africa Prot., 1915; asst. aud. in charge, Zanzibar, Nov., 1915; ag. treas., Zanzibar, 14th Sep., 1919.

PARODI, ERNEST VICTOR.—B. 1870; ed. Stonehurst Coll., Lancs.; called to the bar, Inn. Temp., June, 1893; solr.-gen., N. Nigeria, 4th July, 1903; ag. atty.-gen., 2nd Aug., to 22nd Dec., 1903, 15th Apr. to 21st July, 1904. 1st Mar. to 31st Oct., 1905; 16th June to 20th Oct., 1906; 18th Nov., 1906, to 13th June, 1907, and 13th Feb. to 29th Apr., 1908; ag. chief justice, 30th Apr. to 15th Dec., 1908, and Sept., 1909; puisne judge, 1st Apr., 1910; ag. chief just., 28th Apr. to 25th Nov., 1910, and from 21st Feb. to 20th Sept., 1912; judge of the circuit ct. of the Prot. and puisne judge of the supreme ct. of the col. of Sierra Leone, Apr., 1913; ag. chief just., 22nd Oct. to 9th Nov., 1913, 27th Nov., 1914 to 17th Apr., 1915, and from 19th July, 1916 to 17th Jan., 1917.

PARR, CECIL WILLIAM CHASE.—Jun. offr., Perak civ. ser., 1889; dist. offr., Tampin, Negri Sembilan, 1897; dist. offr., Klang, 1904; ag. comsnr. of trade and customs, F.M.S., 1909; ag. British res., Negri Sembilan, 1911; 2nd lieut. M.S.V.R., 1911; under sec., F.M.S., 1912; lieut. M.S.V.R., 1912; ag. agt., M.S. information agency, London, 1912; seconded as gov. of B. N. Borneo, Apr., 1913; lieut. (remount offr.), 30th June to 7th Dec., 1915; British res., Pahang, 5th Feb., 1916; capt., M.S.V.R., 10th Mar., 1916.
PARRY, C. E.—Tempy. asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., May, 1919.

PARSONS, LAURANCE DUDLEY, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.).—B. 1873; res. surg. and supt., New Providence hosp., Nassau, Bahamas, Sep., 1901, to Dec., 1904; port surg., etc., Gibraltar, Jan., 1905, to Aug., 1912; med. supt., lunatic asylum, Ceylon, Aug., 1912; ag. registr., Ceylon med. coll., in addition to his own duties, Apr., 1917.

PASHLEY, EDWARD REYNOLD.—B. 1869; storekeeper, petroleum warehouse, customs, Bahamas, 1895; landing waiter, 1896; senr. clk. of check staff, 1897; landing waiter and preventive offr., 1899; attended impl. customs, London, 1900; customs offr., excise supervisor, boarding offr., Tobago, 1901; landing waiter, customs, Trinidad, 1902; transf. to acctnt's branch, treasry, 1905; 5th clk. and paymr., 1907; 4th clk., 1912; 3rd clk., 1913; comprtr. of customs, regis. of shipping, J.P., Bahamas, 1914; ag. rec-gen., 7th to 16th Feb., 1919, and from 19th Sep. to 12th Nov., 1919.

PASSINGHAM, FRANK SELWOOD.—B. 1883; ed. Goldsmiths' and Morley Colls., Lond.; hons. medallist in typography, City and Guilds of Lond. Inst.; govt. printer, Mauritius, Feb., 1914; also supt., bookbinding works and stat. stores, Apr., 1914.

PATENAUME, HON. ENIOFF LEON, B.A.—B. 1875; ed. Montreal Coll. and Laval Univ.; advocate, elected to legis. assem., Quebec, 1908; re-elec., 1912; min. of int. rev., Borden ministry, Oct., 1915; elec. by acclamation for Hochelaga, Quebec, 1915; S. of S., 1917; mem. of the P.C. for Canada; resig. portfolio, June, 1917.

PATERNER, GEO. WILLIAM, F.R.I.P.H., mem. B.M.A.—B. 1866; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; St. Mary's Hosp., London; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., London, Jan., 1890; house surgeon, St. Mary's Hosp., London, Mar.-Apr., 1890; ag. med. offr., Leeward dist., St. Vincent, Nov., 1890, to Apr., 1891; med. offr., St. David's, Grenada, May, 1891, to Oct., 1898; house surg., Colony hosp., Grenada, Nov., 1898, to Dec., 1902; med. offr., dist. I., health offr., Port of St. George, operating surg., Colony hosp., 1902-17; col. surg., Grenada, operating surg.; consulting surg. and consulting physician, Colony hosp., medical supt., lunatic and poor asylums, and med. offr. of prisons, 1st July, 1917; J.P., M.L.C., since Mar., 1914.

PATTON, THOMAS WALTERS.—B.A. (Dublin); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; ag. 2nd A.D.O., Tapah, June, 1911; ag. A.D.O., Larut, Oct., 1912; ditto, Gopeng, Jan., 1913; ditto, Larut, Feb., 1913; ditto, Lands, Kinta, July, 1913; passed final exam. in Malay, Dec., 1913; ag. mag., Seremban, Dec., 1913; ag. A.D.O., Raub, Oct., 1914; 2nd lieut., 5th R. Irish Fus., Aug., 1915; attached censor's office, Singapore, May, 1917; ag. D.O., N. Tebal, July, 1917; ag. D.O.B.M., May, 1919.

PATULLO, HON. THOMAS D.—B. 1873; journalist; editor of "The Galt Reformer"; appld. secy. to Comsnr. of the Yukon, 1897; resigned to enter business in Dawson; later, removed to Prince Rupert, where he took an active interest in municipal affairs, at one time holding the office of mayor; elec. to legis. ass., Brit. Columbia, g.e., 1916; min. of lands in Brit. Columbia cabinet, 1916.

PAUL, A. H. D.—Ed. at Bradfield and Bath colls.; trooper in Mataebeland mtd. pol., 1895; took part in the Jameson raid, 1896; planting in Ceylon, 1896 to 1904; trooper in Ceylon M.I.; dist. supt. of pol., N. Nigeria, 1905; asst. res., 1st Nov., 1908; ag. supt. of agric., Aug., 1911.

PAUL, ROBERT JAMES.—B. 1888; sub-inspr., constab., Trinidad, 9th Sept., 1912; 2nd lieut., Trinidad local forces, 29th June, 1913; pay and quartermaster, constab., and senr. supply and transport offr., Trinidad local forces, 21st Nov., 1914, to 9th Oct., 1916, and from 10th Apr. to 31st May, 1917; ag. inspr. of constab. and capt. local forces, 15th Aug., 1915; asst. supt., fire brigade, 24th Nov., 1914, to 14th Nov., 1915, and from 1st Feb. to 31st Mar., 1919; ag. supt., fire brigade, 15th Nov., 1915, to 30th Sep., 1916; J.P. for the colony; ag. chief of police, condit. local forces, sup. of prison, St. Vincent, 4th Aug., 1919; J.P. for the colony.

PAULIN, GEORGE.—B. 1885; ed. Canterbury Coll., Univ. of New Zealand, B.Sc., 1906; B.Eng., 1908; pupilage with govt. rly., New Zealand, with city engrn., Christchurch, and with Selwyn county engrn. and engrn.-in-chief, city of Dunedin drainage; engrn.-surv., P.W.D., N. S. Wales, 1910; dist. engrn., Fiji, 1912; res. engrn., Suva harbr. wks., Fiji, 1915.
PAWAN, JAMES LENOX, M.B., Ch.B., Edin.—B. 1887; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, 1st Jan., 1913; dist. med. offr., 8th Mar., 1916.

PEACOCK, HON. SIR ALEXANDER JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1861; mem. of Austn. Federal Convention, 1897-8; premier of Victoria, 1901-2; later chief sec. and min. for labour, Victoria; min. for educn. and labour, Dec., 1913; premier, treas., and min. of justice, 1914.

PEACOCK, WALTER.—B.A., Trinity Hall, Camb.; cadet, S. Stlnta., Nov., 1898; passed fin. exam. Chinese, June, 1901; ag. 2nd asst. protect. Chinese, Penang, July, 1901; ag. dist. offr., Christmas Is., Dec., 1902; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, June, 1903; appld. to cls. V., Sept., 1903; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, July, 1906; seconded for service in Burma, June, 1907; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Mar., 1909.

PEARCE, DR. ALEXANDER HUGH BLAXELL.—B. 1882; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edin. Univ. Trin. Coll., Dublin; L.R.C.P. & S., Edin.; L.R.F.P. & S., Glasgow (1904); L.M., Dublin (1906); D.P.H., Dublin Univ., 1918; spec. certif., London Schol. of Trop. Med.; F.R.I. Pub. Health; Fellow of Tropical Society; ag. R.M.O., Seamen's Hosp., Albert Docks; house surg., Lincoln County Hosp.; C.C., Coombe lying-in hosp., Dublin; junr. med. offr., Mont errat, 1907; D.M.O., St. Kitts; res. med. supt., Charitable Instit., Antigua, and health offr., Antigua, 1908; ag. C.G.M.O., Antigua, 1911; col. surg., Falkland Is., 1912; M.L.C. and M.E.C., Falkland Is., dormant comm., to administer govt. of Falklands; pres., bd. of health, Falklands; seconded to R.A.M.C., 1916-1918; capt., R.A.M.C.; served in France and Far East; chief med. offr., Bahamas, 1918; M.L.C.; chmn., hosp. bd., Bahamas Genl. Hosp.

PEARCE, MAJOR FRANCIS BARROW, C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1866; ed. at Cheltenham, and Roy. Mil. Coll., Sandhurst; entd. W. Yorks. regt., 1886; capt., 1893; brevet-major 1900; major, 1904; served with Ashanti expdn., 1896-6 (star) in B. C. Africa, 1898 (despa., medal with clasp); in commd. of British forces in operation in E. Nyasaland, 1899 (despa., medal with clasp, brevet major); and in S. Africa, 1901 (medal with clasp); asst. dep. comsnr., B.C. Africa Prot., 1st Nov., 1897; dep. comsnr., 1901; acted as comsnr., and consul-gen., 1903, Nov., 1906, to Jan., 1906, and during 1907; ag. gov., Nyasaland, Apr.-July, 1910; and from Nov., 1912 to Sept., 1913; British res., Zanzibar Prot., 9th Feb., 1914; ag. high comsnr., 10th Apr., 1917, to 30th Jan., 1919, and from 3rd Dec., 1919; author of "Zanzibar: the Island Metropolis of Eastern Africa."

PEARCE, HON. GEORGE FOSTER.—Senator for Western Australia in commonwealth parlt.; sec. of trades coun., Perth, 1896; pres., 1899; returned to first Commonwealth Senate, 1901; re-elected, 1906, and elected chmn. of comtes.; min. of defence, C. of A., Apr., 1910 to June, 1913; at Imp. Conf., 1911; min. of defence since Sept., 1914.

PEARCE, THOS. WM.—B. 1855; Missionary of Lond. Miss. Soc., Canton, 1873-1890; Hong Kong, 1890-1902; ag. inspr. of schools, 5th Sept. to 3rd Oct., 1898; again in 1900; examiner, Queen's Coll., 1897 and 1898; mem. of comtee. on interpretation, 1899-1900; apptd. mem. of bd. of examiners in Chinese, 14th Jan., 1897; mem. of govn. body, Queen's Coll., 1905; mem. of comtee. on continuation classes, 1907.

PEARSE, WILFRID WILLIAM.—M.B., C.M., Aberdeen, 1893; D.P.H., Aberdeen, 1904; M.D., Aberdeen, 1906; 2nd asst. med. offr., Shoreditch Infirmary, 1894-95; asst. med. offr. of health, Hong Kong, 1901; J.P., 1902; ag. med. offr. of health, 1903-6, 1908 and 1915; med. offr. of health, 1915; lecturer on pub. health, Univ. of Hong Kong.

PEARSON, ARTHUR A., C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1847; ed. at Rugby; apptd., 1867, after a compet. exam., clk. in the office of the S. of S. for cols.; 2nd class clk., Sept., 1872; asst. priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, Mar., 1874, to Feb., 1878; asst. priv. sec. to Sir M. Hicks-Beach, Feb. to Mar., 1878; priv. sec. to Earl Cadogan, July, 1879, to Apr., 1880; priv. sec. to the Right Hon. M. E. Grant Duff, Apr., 1880, to Dec., 1880; 1st-class clk., Jan., 1881; principal clk., Apr., 1885; repres. crown cols. at postal confere., London, June and July, 1898; one of the Brit. delegates at the Brussels sugar conference, 1901-2; asst. delegate, permanent sugar commission at Brussels, 1903; ret. from C.O., 31st Dec., 1906; accompanied Sir C. Lucas on his visit to Australia and N. Zealand, 1909.

PEARSON, AYLMER CAVENDISH, C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1876; ed. at Felsted schl., Essex, and Trinity coll., Dublin; cadet, N. Borneo serv., 1897; ast. treas., 1899; res., Kudat, 1901; sec. to gov., 1904; sessions judge, 1905; mem. of coun., 1906; ag. judcl. comsnr., 1908; ag. comsnr. of land., 1908; gov't. sec. and judge of high ct., 1909; ag. gov', N. Borneo, 1910 and 1911; temporarily attached to C.O. for a short period in 1912, after which he returned to Borneo as dep. gov. for 3 months; attached to various colonial services for short periods; gov', B. N. Borneo, 1915.
PEARSON, ERNEST A.—Asst. master, training coll., Accra, G. Coast, 13th Dec., 1911; acted as principal ditto, and as instr. and provincial instr. of schls. on several occasions.
PEARSON, H.—Tempy. asst. dist. comnr., E.A.P., Aug., 1919.
PEARSON, J. M.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 14th Apr., 1909; dist. comsnr., Dec., 1917.
PEARSON, JOSEPH, D.Sc. (Liverpool), B.S. (Victoria), F.L.S.—Dir. of the Colombo Museum, and marine biologist to Ceylon Govt., 11th Aug., 1910.
PEASE, J. W. K.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914.

PEDLOW, JOSEPH HOWARD, B.A. (N.U.I.).—B. 1889; ed. Queen's Coll., Galway; cadet, S. Stiltns., Dec., 1912; attached Chinese Prot., Penang, Jan., 1913; at Amoy to study Chinese (Hok-kien), Sep., 1913; attached Chinese Prot., Singapore, Mar., 1916; attached treasy. for custodian enemy property and war loan work, May, 1916; collr. of war tax, Singapore, in addition, Mar., 1918.
PEDRAZA, R.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Aug., 1914.

PEEBLES, MAJOR H. W., D.S.O. (1917).—Joined Canadian M.I., Dec., 1899; 2nd lieut. A.S.C., Sept., 1900; lieut., Nov., 1901; S. African War, Jan., 1900, to May, 1902 (severely wounded, Queen's medal and four clasps, King's medal and two clasps); res. of offrs., June, 1902; A.D.C. to Brig.-Gen. Sir W. H. Manning, K.C.M.G., C.B., Feb., 1903, to June, 1904; Somaliland campaign, Jan., 1903, to June, 1904 (twice men. in deep, medal and two clasps); A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Sir B. Sweet-Escott, K.C.M.G., gov'r. of B. Hond., Oct., 1904; local capt., Nov., 1904; commndg. Belize L.I. volrs., Dec., 1904; local major, 1905; ast. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1905 to Jan., 1907; ag. priv. sec. to Sir B. B. Sweet-Escott, K.C.M.G., gov'r. of Leeward Is., Oct., 1907; ast. res., N. Nigeria, Mar., 1908; 3rd class res., N. Nigeria, Jan., 1909; resigned on account of ill-health, June, 1909; clk. and priv. sec. to administrator, Dominica, Mar., 1913; raised and commndg. Dom. Defence For., Aug., 1914; served with B.E.F. in France from Oct., 1914; ment. in desps., Jan., 1916, Jan., 1917, and May, 1917; awarded D.S.O., June, 1917; ment. in desps., June, 1919; brevet major, June, 1919; D.A.D.O.S., 3rd Army, B.E.F., June, 1916, to Oct., 1917; D.A.D.O.S., Aldershot Command, May, 1919, to 31st May, 1919; comsnr., Virgin Is'ands, July, 1919.

PEEL, W.—B.A., Queen's Coll., Cambridge, 11th wrangler, math. trip., 1896; cadet, S. Stiltns., Oct., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Aug.-Dec., 1898; and from Mar., 1899; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Dec., 1899; dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1901; ag. 2nd ast. col. soc., May, 1902; ag. ast. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Nov., 1902, to Jan., 1903; and in Aug., 1903; ag. asst. supt., Indian immigrts., Sing., in addition, Aug., 1903; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Sept., 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Feb., 1905; ag. collr. of land rev., Penang, Jan., 1906; dist. offr., Tampin, F.M.S. June, 1908; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, 22nd Oct., 1909; ag. dist. offr., Lower Perak, 21st Feb., 1910; ag. supt. of Indian immigrts., S.S. and F.M.S., 2nd May, 1910; ag. chief asst. dist. offr., Kinta, and regrist. of titles, South Perak, 21st Oct., 1910; seconded as pres. mun. comsnnr., Penang, 1st Jan., 1911; promoted to Class III., but remained seconded, 1st Jan., 1912; acted as res. councillor, Penang, in addtn., Feb. to Oct., 1917; pres., mun. comsnnr., Singapore, July, 1918; offr. of Class II., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1919; joint passage controller, Malaya, in addition, Feb., 1919; food controller, Malaya, 26th May, 1919.

PEET, JAMES HERBERT.—B. 1882; elk., audit office, Bahamas, 1904; 4th elk., P.O., 1907; 2nd elk., C.S.O., 1909; 3rd elk., treasy. dept., 1910; ch. elk., P.O., 1913; ch. elk. and asst. examg. offr., customs, 1914; has held numerous ag. appointments.

PELLETIER, HON. LOUIS PHILIPPE, K.C.—B. 1857; ed., St. Anne Coll., Laval Univ., Quebec; pres. of Nat. conservative assocn. of prov. of Quebec; founded newspaper "La Justice"; leg. coun., Quebec, 1888; res., and ret., by acclam. to legis. assembly for Dorchester; prov. sec., 1891; atty.-gen., 1896-7; sat for Dorchester until 1904; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1911; mem. of the P.C. for Canada, and postmaster-gen. in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; judge, superior court, Quebec, 1914; ct. of King's Bench, 1916.

PELLETIER, PHILIPPE.—B. 1849; called to the bar, Quebec, 1875; mem. of the coun. of the bar of Montreal, and sec., 1878; a fire comsnnr. for city of Montreal, 1879 to 1887; unsuccessfully contested St. John's in provincial elections, 1886, and Beauharnois in Dominion elections, 1887; ch. elk., sec. of state's dept., Canada, 1888; asst.-under-sec. of state of Canada, 1909.

PEMBERTON, SHOLTO RAWLINS.—Ed. Downing Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1882; called to the bar (Inner Tem.), 1883; admitted to the bar, Leeward Is., 1883; elected mem. legis. assem., Dominica, and of gen. legis. coun., Leewards, 1885 to 1895; mem. educn. coman., 1886; of road bd., Dominica, 1889; nominated mem. legis. assem., Dominica, and mem. exec. coun., 1895; comsnnr. of valuation, 1893; comsnnr. of assessed taxes, 1896; J.P., 1896; chmn. bd. of guardians, 1897; vis. just. of gaol, 1897; mem. quarantine bd., 1898; nom. mem. legis. coun., Dominica, and of gen. legis. coun., Leewards, 1898; ag. 1st puisne judge, Leewards, 1898; ag. asst. atty.-gen., Dominica, 1899-1900; ag. 2nd puisne judge, 1901 and 1902; asst. atty.-gen., 1903; ag. puisne judge, 1903-4-5-7-8-9 and 1910; ag. atty.-gen., 1905, 1908, 1909 and 1910; ag. chief just., 1911; ag. admstr. of Dominica, 1911; 2nd puisne judge, Leeward Is., Apr., 1911; 1st puisne judge, Mar. 1916; ag. chief just., May, 1916.

PENNEFATHER, CHARLES EDWARD DE FONBLANCQUE.—B. 1885; elk., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Prot., 1908; elk. of peace, 1910; sub-inspr. of constabty., Fiji, 1910; inspr., ditto, 1912; dist. comsnnr., 1914; ag. dist. comsnnr., Rewa, and gov. comsnnr., Naitasiri, 1914-15; ag. dist. comsnnr., Colo East, 1917-18; ag. ch. inspr., Fiji constabty., ag. ch. pol. mag., regist.-gen., regist. of sup. ct., regist. of titles, pub. trustee, regist. of companies, cust. of enemy property, and cur. of intestate and vacant estates, 1918.
PENNEY, ALFRED.—M.H.A., Carbonear, Newfild., 1885-9; mem. exec. coun. and survr.-gen., 1885-9; judge dist. ct. Harbour Grace, 1898; stip. mag., Carbonear, 1900.

PEPPYS, WALTER EVELYN.—B. 1885; ed. at Malvern and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1907; M.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1908; ag. asst. dist. offr., Pekan, Apr., 1910; ag. asst. sec. to res., Perak, Jan., 1911; passed cadet, June 1911; cls. V., Jan., 1912; dist. offr., Jelebu, Apr., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, June, 1913; ag. mag., cent. ct., Kelantan, and state auditor, Mar., 1914; on duty with Kelantan detachmt., Malay States Guides, 15th to 31st May, 1915; dist. offr., Pasir Puteh, 1st June, 1915; ag. supt. of lands, Kelantan, and chmn., conservancy bd., Aug. to Dec., 1917; dist. offr., Pasir Puteh, Jan., 1918; ag. supt. of lands, Kelantan and chmn., conservancy bd. in addition, May, 1918.

PERCEVAL, SIR WESTBY BROOK, K.C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1854; was mem. for Christchurch City in legis. assem., of N. Zealand; chmn. of comtees. in 1890; agt.-gen. for the col. in England, 1891-96; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1878; Roy. comsnnr. for Chicago Exhibn., 1893; agt.-gen. for Tasmania, 1896 to 1899.
PERCIVAL, ARTHUR BLAYNEY.—Asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 14th June, 1900; ranger of game preserves, 10th May, 1901; senr. asst. game warden, 1910; game warden, June, 1915.

PERCY, FREDERICK WM.—B. 1857; ed. at private schl., Dublin; writer, Royal Navy, 1872; passed English civ. serv. exam., 2nd cls. elk., 1874; elk., wks. and rlwys. depts., W. Australia, 1892; minister's elk., wks. dept., 1896; ag. chf. elk., Jan., 1898; statistical clk., July, 1898; ag. chf. elk., Jan. to Dec., 1902; chf. correspondence elk., Mar., 1903; ag. chf. elk. for pub. wks., 14th Nov., 1908.

PERDUE, CECIL GRAHAM.—B. 1891; ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; police probationer, Hong Kong, Nov., 1912; attached to Punjab police, Apr., 1915; temp. 2nd lieut., Indian army reserve, 27th Jan., 1916; ag. asst. supt. police, Hong Kong, 1919; ag. deputy supt. police, Sept.-Oct., 1919.

PERDUE, HON. WM. EGERTON, B.A.—B. 1850; ed. Brampton high schl. and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, Ontario, 1879; called to the bar, Manitoba, 1882; judge, ct. of appeal, Manitoba, 1903; ch. just. of Manitoba, 1918.
PERERA, THEODORE DUNCAN.—B. 1895; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., local divn., Aug., 1919; attached to Kurunegala Kach., Aug., 1919; Jaffna Kach., Dec., 1919.
PREZ, JOSEPH ALDRIC, M.D. EDIN.—B. 1860; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, 6th Apl., 1887; dist. med. offr., 1st Mar., 1915.

PERIES, FRANCIS DANIEL.—B. 1864; ed. Royal coll., Colombo; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., 1899; pol. mag., S. Prov., Jan., 1904, Apr., 1905; Mar., 1907; ag. comsnnr. of requests and pol. mag., Galle, Mar., 1905; ag. pol. mag., Balapitiya, Oct., 1906; pol. mag., Chilaw, July, 1910; pol. mag., Panadura, Feb., 1912; ag. dist. jud., Tangalla, Oct., 1913; dist. jud., Ratnapura, July, 1917.

PERKINS, ARTHUR JAMES.—B. 1871; Diplômé de l'école Nationale d'Agriculture de Montpellier, 1890; management of farm estates, Tunis, 1890-2; prof. of viticulture and oenology, S. Aust., 1892; sec. for agriculture, 1902-1904; prin. of agric. coll. and prof. of viticulture and oenology, 1904; dir. of agric., 1914.

PERKINS, LEICESTER GRANT.—B. 1890; ed. privately and Harrison Coll., Barbados; clk., post office, Barbados, Jan., 1909; 5th clk., audit office, Mar., 1911; 2nd clk., registn. office, Nov., 1913; clk. to master in chancery, May, 1914; 4th clk., savings bank, Jan., 1915; 2nd-lieut., Imperial Army, 30th Mar., 1917, served during the war in Egypt and Palestine with 2nd B.W.I. Regt.; 1st-lieut., 1st Oct., 1918; 1st clk., Barbados savings bank, Aug., 1919; 2nd grade supvr. of customs, Nigeria, Feb., 1920.

PERKINS, THOS. LUFT, A.M. Inst. C.E., A.R.I.B.A.—Divtn. survr. and engrnr. for county of Middlesex; dist. engrnr. for City dist. of Bristol; ditto for Clifton dist., Bristol; exec. engrnr. P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1904; 1st asst., D.P.W., 1918.

PERLEY, HON. SIR GEORGE HALSEY, K.C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1857; ed. Ottawa gram. schl., St. Paul's schl., Concord, N.H., and Harvard Univ.; B.A., 1878; elec. to H. of C. Canada (Argenteuil) 1904; re-elee., 1908 and 1911; chief conservative whip, 1911; mem. of privy coun. for Canada and min. without portfolio in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; ag. high commr. in London, 1914; min. of overseas mil. forces, 1916; high commr., 1917; joint commr. and plenipotentiary of Canada to sign Peace Treaties, 1919.

PERRY, SIR ALLAN, KT. BACH. (1904): M.D. (Durham); D.P.H.(Lon.); M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.S.A. Lond.; B.1860; prin. civ. med.offr. and inspr.-gen. of hosps., Ceylon; prin. of Ceylon med. coll., 10th Dec., 1897; major R.A.M.C. (retired); mem. municipal coun., Colombo; late pres. Ceylon branch Jir. med. assoc.; del. for Ceylon to Internat. Leprosy Conf., Bergen, 1909; ret., 1915.
PERRY, B. C. G.—B.1886; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to C.O., 9th Apr., 1906.

PERRY, GEORGE McREDDIE, M.B., C.M., Glasgow, and St. Mary's hosp., London, F.R.G.S.—Asst. col. surg., Lagos, Oct., 1893; ag. col. surg., Feb. Mar., 1894; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1894; med. offr. to exped. to Italan mountains; sen. asst. col. surg., Aug., 1895; ag. col. surg., 1895.

PERRY-LEWIS, CAPT. GEORGE, F.S.S.; B. 1830; entd. Imp. postal serv., 1st July, 1896; transfld. to post and telegraph dept., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 3rd Nov., 1905; asst. P.M.G., N. Nigeria, 27th Apr., 1910; ag. dep. P.M.G., Jan., 1913; 1st cls. postmtr., E.A.P., Nov., 1913; ag. asst. P.M.G., Nov., 1916; asst. P.M.G., Mar., 1918; passed govt. test in Swahili language; lieut., E. Africa (field) telegraph sect., Nov., 1916; ag. capt. and offr. commdng., July, 1918.
PERTH, ARCHBISHOP OF, MOST REV. CHAS. OWEN LEAVER RILEY, D.D., Cantab.—B. 1854; vicar of St. Paul's, Preston, Lancs., 1885-1894; bishop of Perth, W. Aust., 1894; created archbishop, 1914.
PETCH, TOM, B.A., B.Sc., (Lond.).—B. 1870; botanist and mycologist, Ceylon, 1905; ag. dir. of agric., 1914, 1915 and 1916.

PETRIDES, PHILIP BERTR.—B. 1881; ed. Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1906; joined Midland Circ. and Birmingham and cent. crim. court sess., 1906; legal adviser and crown prosecutor, Seychelles, 22nd Jan., 1916; ex-officio mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ag. chief just., 23rd July, 1916 to 20th Mar., 1917.

PETTIT, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.I. Mech. E.—B. 1866; ed. Battersea Gram. Schl.; served pupillage with Messrs. John Aird and Sons, 1881-1886; engrnr. and man. in charge of construc. of various pub. wks., in U.K., 1886 to 1910; chief asst. engrnr., govt. water works, G. Coast, 1910; ag. res. engrnr. on ditto, 1910 to 1916; dep. D.P.W., G. Coast, Feb., 1916; ag. D.P.W., Mar. to Sept., 1916, and from 4th Oct. to 13th Dec., 1916; temporarily attached to Ministry of Munitions in England from 22nd Feb., 1917, to 31st Jan., 1918; ag. dir. of pub. wks., and M.L.C., 28th Mar., 1918.

PHELIPS, HUGH RICHARD.—Ed. Weymouth Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; local auditor, Niger Coast Prot., 27th Oct., 1894; asst. auditor, E. Africa Prot., 9th Dec., 1896; local auditor, Uganda, 29th Apr., 1897; served in Uganda mutiny (medal and clasp); local auditor, E. Africa and Uganda rly., 26th Dec., 1901; auditor, Hong Kong, 1st Nov., 1904; J.F., 1905; hon. auditor, Hong Kong Univ., 2nd May, 1911.

PHILBRICK, ARTHUR JAMES.—Ed. Rugby, 1879-1884, Trin. Coll., Camb., 1884-1887; B.A.; LL.B., Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Temp., 1889; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, 16th Nov., 1901; prov. commr., 14th Apr., 1902; prov. commr., Ashanti, 1900; ag. chief commr., Ashanti, 1911, 1912 and 1914; attached to War Trade dept., London, Feb.-Sept., 1915, and from May to Sept., 1917.

PHILIPPS, JAMES ERASMUS TRACY, Capt. R. of O., M.C. (1916), B.A., B. Litt., F.R.G.S., F.R.A.I.—B. 1888; ed. Marlborough Coll., Oxford and Durham Univ., of which was undergraduate representative at the Congress of Univ. of the Empire, London, 1912; editor of Durham Univ. Journal; hon. sec., D.U.B.C.; pres. of the Union; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, 27th Mar., 1913; asst. intelligence offr., E. Africa Expedy. Force, from 8th Aug., 1914; commanding 2,000 armed levies and scouts, from 9th Sept., 1914 (wounded and ment. in desps.); adminstr. Kifumbire (Bukoba) area of occupied territory from 5th Sept., 1914, under martial law; polit. offr. on staff of Major-Gen. J. M. Stewart, C.B., Bukoba Expedy. Forces, from 15th June, 1915 (ment. in desps.); chief polit. offr. Uganda area, including Kagera mil. dist., from 17th Jan., 1916; chief intelligence offr. (C.S.O.3) on staff of the Lake Command, E.A.E.F., to Tabora, from 4th Aug., 1916; in charge of polit. mission in British and Belgian areas of conquered territory, Nov., 1916 to Mar., 1917, when invalided; attached Admiralty War Staff, intell. div., June, 1917; employed by F.O. on mission to Abyssinia (attached Rifle Brigade), Jan., 1918; on special duty in Sudan, Jan., 1918; employed on Sudan frontier in connection with Sudan-Turkana operations, May, 1918; ag. dist. cmsg., Kigezi, British Ruanda, and dist. mag. Kabale, Feb., 1919; publications, "Pan-Islam in Africa," "Cape to Cairo developments," "Ruanda" (1919), "The Nabingi" (1919).

PHILLIPS, HAROLD ERNEST.—B. 1877; temporary clk. to auditor, B. Honduras, 6th Oct., 1892; 5th clk., treas., customs and P.O. depts., 23rd June, 1893; 4th clk., 1st Feb., 1894; 3rd clk., treas. and cust. dept., 16th May, 1897; 2nd clk., 7th June, 1893; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1st Sept., 1900; ag. chief clk., C.S.O., and clk. of councils, 12th Apr. to 30th Sept., 1901, 15th Mch., 1902, to 24th July, 1904, and from 14th Dec., 1904, to 28th June, 1905; ag. dist. commr., Stann Creek, 32nd July, 1905, to 14th Jan., 1906; ch. clk., C.S.O. and clk. of couns., 17th Jan., 1906; priv. sec. to gov. Sir E. Swayne, 16th Mch., 1909, to 31st July, 1910; ag. commr. of currency, 18th Feb., 1911; ag. treas., collr. of cust., harb. mast., regis. of shipping, chmn., of bd. for examtn. of pilots, pres. of quarantine bd., chmn. of poor relief committee, dir. of P.O.G. fund, mem. of bd. of educn.; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 9th Apr. to 13th Aug., 1911; J.P. for the Colony, 8th May, 1911, priv. sec. to ag. gov., 5th Aug., 1911.

PHILLIPS, HENRY DENBIGH.—B. 1872; supernum. clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, 1887; ag. clk., col. engrnr.'s office, 1888; ag. rev. offr., July, 1888, to Feb., 1889; clk. to att.-gen., May, 1889; ag. clk. to regnr. of sup. ct. and keeper of records, 1889, 1890, 1891, 1892, 1893 and 1894; clk. to regnr., sup. ct., 1894; comsnr. of sup. ct. and a comsnr. of deeds, Aug., 1894; ag. registgen., Feb. to Sept., 1898; ag. dist. comsnr., Stann Creek, Apr. to Oct., 1899; ditto, Toledo, Mar. to Dec., 1900; dist. comsnr., Toledo, 1st Jan., 1901; dist. comsnr., Stann Creek, 28th Aug., 1906; ag. dist. comsnr., Belize; offl. recvr. in bankruptcy and offl. admstr., 11th Mar., 1907; ag. registgen. in conjunction with duties as dist. comsnr., Apr. to Dec., 1907; and again from 5th Apr. to 22nd Oct., 1908; a comsnr. of currency, 24th Sep., 1908; ag. treas. and collr. of cust., harb.-master, etc., pres., quarantine bd., chmn. of pauper relief comtce., 23rd Oct., 1908, to May, 1909; ag. dist. comsnr., Belize, etc., and regist.-gen., 21st May to 12th Aug., 1909, and from 22nd Jan., 1910, to 3rd Dec., 1911; in charge of audit dept., 4th Dec., 1911.

PHILLIPS, WILLIAM LAMBERT COLLIER, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1858; B.A. (Dunelm); dep. clk., gen. assembly, Barbados, 1879; comsnr. of probate, 1882; clk. gen. assem., 1891; col. treasnr., 1899; ag. col. sec., July to Sept., 1911, May to Aug., 1912, and from May to Dec., 1913.
PHILLIPSON, SYDNEY.—B. 1892; cadet Ceylon civil serv., Nov., 1919; attchd. to Kandy Kehi., Dec., 1919.

PHILIP, HON. SIR ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1915). —B. 1851; M.L.A. of Queensland, 1886; sec. for pub. wks., 1893-6; sec. for pub. instr., 1894-5; for rlyws., 1895-7; for mines, 1893-9; treasnr., Mar., 1898, to Dec., 1899; premier, Dec., 1899, to Sept., 1903; and Nov., 1907, to Feb., 1908.
PICKERING, G. H.—Town mag., E.A.P., 1910; ag. 2nd puisne judge 1915; 2nd puisne judge, 1917.
PICKFORD, W.—Dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 21st Aug., 1909.

PICKWOAD, KEUL ARTHUR.—B. 1876; ed. Bedford Modern schl.; 3rd customs offr. Br. Hond., June, 1895; 2nd ditto, Nov., 1895; several times acted as clk. in treasry., cust. and post offr.; 3rd clk., treasry., and cust. and clk. to quarantine bd., May, 1897; 2nd clk., Sept., 1900; astt. auditor, N. and S. Nigeria, Dec., 1901; local auditor, G. Coast, Dec., 1902; local auditor, Lagos-S. Nigeria, May, 1905; ditto, new col. and prot. of S. Nigeria, 1906; ditto, Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1914.

PICKWOAD, HOWELL.—Ed. at Bedford Mod. schl.; clk., atty.-gen.'s offr., Br. Hond., Nov., 1895; 3rd cust. offr., 12th Aug., 1898; 4th clk., treasry. and cust., and clk. to comsnrs. of currency, 27th Dec., 1901; 2nd grade cust. and excise offr., Aug., 1903; col. branch of exchequer and audit dept., 21st Oct., 1904; astt. treas., E. Africa Prot., 26th June, 1905.
PICKWOAD, HUGH FREDERICK.—B. 1878; comsnr. (4th div.), Bahamas, 1909; ditto (3rd div.), 1914.
PIDDINGTON, ALFRED BATHURST, K.C.—B. 1862; chief comsnr. of the interstate comsnr., Commonwealth of Australia, Aug., 1913.

PIERIS, PAULUS EDWARD, Samarasingha Sriwardhana, M.A., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1895.— Barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 1896; astt. to govt. agt., Colombo, 1898; pol. mag., Panadura, 1899; dist. judge, Ratnapura, 1899; astt. to govt. agt., Galle, 1901; ag. dist. judge, Matara, Mar., 1903; office astt. to govt. agt., Galle, June, 1903; astt. comsnr., St. Louis exhibn., Feb., 1904; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1905; ditto, Kalutara, Mar., 1906; ag. registr.-gen., Dec., 1910; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, May, 1912; comsnr. of requests, (Colombo, Nov., 1912; ag. dist. judge, Kandy, Nov., 1913; dist. judge, Galle, June, 1914; ditto, Jaffna, Dec., 1915; ag. dist. judge, Colombo, Mar., 1919.

PIGGOTT, SIR FRANCIS TAYLOR, KT. BACH. (1905).—Barrister-at-law; employed by F.O. on special service in 1887; legal adv. to prim. min., Japan, Nov., 1887; sec. to att.-gen. Sir C. Russell (afterwards Lord Russell of Killowen) during Behring Sea arbitrn., 1893-4; proc.-gen., Mauritius, 1894; ag. ch. just., 1895 to 1897; ch. just., Hong Kong, 27th Apr., 1906; ret., 1912; recd. thanks of coun. of govt. for ser. in connection with planters' loans, 1902; author of "Nationality and Naturalization," "Exterritoriality," "Extradition," "Foreign Judgments and Jurisdiction," "Law of Torts," and "Imperial Statutes applicable to the Colonies."

PIGOTT, FRANCIS JOSEPH, M.I.C.E.—Dist. engnr., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1887; ag. mun. engnr., Colombo, 1897; prov. engnr., S. Prov., 1902; ditto, E. Prov., 1903; astt. D.P.W., 1904; dep. col. engnr. and survr.-gen., Penang, S. Stilimta, 1905; ag. col. engnr. and survr.-gen., S.S., Jan., 1907, to Apr., 1908; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Singapore, 1907; ag. col. engnr. and survr.-gen., Sept., 1909; confirmed, Dec.,
PILBEAM, S. A.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., Jan., 1915; passed astt. clk.'s exam., July, 1916; on military service from 18th July, 1917, to 10th Mar., 1919; assigned to C.O. as astt. clk., Apr.,
PIRIE, J. H. H., M.B., Bac. Surg., M.D., M.R.C.P.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1913.

PITOT, LEON EMILE.—B. 1866; govt. survr. and supt. of pub. wks., Seychelles, 11th Apr., 1889; govt. engnr. and architect, Mauritius, Apr., 1891; gen. man. of rlyws. and engineer, Mauritius, 14th Sept., 1896.

PITTMAN, EDWARD FISHER, A.R.S.M.— B. 1849; entd. mines dept., N.S. Wales, 1877; held positions of mining survr., geological survr., and chief mining survr.; lecr. in mining at Sydney Univ., 1893-1902; under-sec. formines, N.S. Wales since Sept., 1902, and govt. geologist from 1901 to 1917; author of "The Mineral Resources of New South Wales," 1901, and many geological reports and papers.

PLANT, GEORGE FREDERIC.—B. 1877; ed. Manchester Gram. Schl. and Brasenose Coll. (scholar, 1896); 1st cl. class. mods., 1895; B.A., 1900; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 1901; off. astt., Kurunegala, 1902; off. astt., Galle, 1904; pol. mag., Avissawella, 1905; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Apr., 1906; ditto, Matara, Dec., 1906; astt. govt. agt., Kalutara, May, 1909; temporarily employed in C.O. London, May, 1915, to Dec., 1918; sec., oversea, settlement comtce., Jan., 1919; permanently transferred to Home civ. serv., Sept.,
PLATTS, W.A.F.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 15th Feb., 1907; dist. comsnr., Apr., 1913.
PLOWMAN, CLIFFORD H. FITZHERBERT.—B. 1889; ed. at King's Sch., Ely, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1911 (class tripes); asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912.

PLOWMAN, Hon. GEORGE THOMAS, C.M.G. (1908).—C.lk. and shorthand writer, col. sec.'s office, Natal, 1890; 1st clk., 1891; ch. clk., 1894; sec. to civ. ser. bd.; asst. under-sec., 1898; sec. to prime min., July, 1901; sec. lands and works (now rlys. and harbrs.), Nov., 1901; mem. civ. serv. bd., 1901; sec. to prime min.; acting offr., rlys. and harbrs., 1st July, 1905; prov. sec., Natal Prov., 1910; dep. adminstr., Natal Prov., Aug., Nov., 1913, and from Aug., 1917; mem. prov. adminstr. comm., 1915; adminstr., Natal, 1918.

PLUMER, FIELD-MARSHAL RT. HON. BARON, SIR HERBERT CHARLES ONSLOW PLUMER, G.C.B. (1918), G.C.M.G. (1916), G.C.V.O. (1917), A.D.C. —B. 1857; served in Soudan expedit., 1884; 4th cls. Medjidie, operations in S. Africa, 1896; S. African War, 1899-1902; A.D.C. to King Edward VII., 1901-2; Q.M.G. to the forces, 1904-5; comdg. 5th divn., Irish command, 1906-9; G.O.C.-in-C., Northern command, 1911-14; comdg. 2nd Army, British exped. force, France, 1915-17; G.O.C.-in-C., British forces in Italy, 1917; gov., Malta, 10th June, 1919; gen., 1915; A.D.C. Gen. to the King, 1917; col., York and Lancs. Regt., 1917; Grand Officer, Legion of Honour; Grand Officer, ss. Maur. and Laz. (Italy); Grand Officer, Order of Leopold, and Croix de Guerre (Belgium).
POILLOANA, CHARLES ADRIEN.—B. 1862; entered civ. serv., Trinidad, 27th Mar., 1882; asst. rec-gen., 1st Apr., 1907.

POMAIRE, HON. MAUI, M.D., Chicago, U.S.A.—B. 1876; educ. Boys' High Sch., Christchurch; govt. health offr. to maoris, 1901; entd. New Zealand parlmt., 1911; mem. of exec. coun., representing native race, 1912; also mem. of Cook Islands.

PONTIFEX, SYDNEY CLAUDE OVERTON.—B. 1871; apptd. to cust., Br. Guiana, 1891; 5th class offr., 1892; 4th ditto, 1894; 3rd ditto, 1898; transf. to cust., So. Nig., 1899; ag. P.M.G., Apr., 1900; asst. collr. cust., Feb., 1901; ag. collr. and ag. P.M.G., July, 1902, to Feb., 1903, and Apr. to Nov., 1904; prov. collr. of cust., Oct., 1906.
POOLE, H.—B. 1887; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., May, 1907.
POOLE, THOMAS STANLEY.—Puisme judge, supreme ct., S. Australia, 1919.

POPE, FREDERICK SIDNEY.—B. 1869; educ. Wellington Boys' Coll., New Zealand; served in govt. rly. dept., N. Zeal.:and, 1886-1900; d-p.t. of agric., 1900-1909; sec. for agriculture, industries and commerce, 1909-1918; asst. director-gen. of agric., 1918.

POPE, SIR JOSEPH, K.C.M.G. (1912), C.V.O. (1908), C.M.G. (1901), I.S.O. (1906).—B. 1854; entered civ. ser. of Canada in 1878 as priv. sec. to min. of marine and fisheries priv. sec. to the late Sir John A. Macdonald, prime min. from 1882 till his death in 1891; asst. clk. of the Queen's Privy Coun. for Canada, 1889; under-sec. of state and dep. regist.-gen. of Canada, 1896; author of the Memoirs of Sir John A. Macdonald; Royal Tour in Canada, 1901, and other works; attached to the staff of the Br. agt. on the Behring Sea arbitration at Paris, 1893; agt. of Canadian govt., at joint high comm., Quebec and Washington, 1898-99; deputies by govt. to arrange Royal tour in Canada, 1901; assoc. sec. to Alaska boundary tribunal, London, 1903; apptd., in association with Mr. Bryce, H.M.'s plenipotentiary at the International Conference on pelagic sealing, 1911; reed. C.V.O. for services on the occasion of the Quebec tercentenary celebrations, 1908; holds 2nd cls. of Japanese Order of the Sacred Treasure; under-sec. of state for external affairs, 1909.
POPE, PERCY W. T.—B. 1856; ed. at Prince of Wales Coll., Charlottetown, P. E. Is.; apptd. Dominion auditor at Charlottetown, 1883; asst. rec-gen., 1st Jan., 1887.

POPE, TREVELYAN ARNOLD.—B. 1860; B.A., Madras Univ., 1876; at Wren's Coll., London, 1877-1879; math. scholarship at Christ's Coll., Camb., 1879; senr. optime, 1882; mast. for two years at Oratory Sch., Birmingham; German and math. mast., Birkenhead Sch., 1884-1887; prof. of physics, Royal Coll., Mauritius, 1887-1904; principal, Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, since 1904; deleg. to agricultural and educational conferece, at Barbados, 1908.

POPHAM, HENRY BRADSHAW, M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1881; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; commn., 13th Light Infantry, 1900; served in S. African War (2 medals); seconded G. Coast Regt., 1906; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to ag. govr., G. Coast, Mar. to Aug., 1909; col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1910; priv. sec. to ag. govr., Feb. to June, 1911; ag. chief asst. col. sec. and clk. of councils, July-Aug., 1913; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., Oct., 1913; dist. commr., Jan., 1914; seconded to Togoland political adminstr., Nov., 1914.

PORCH, MONTAGU PHIPPEN.—B. 1877; ed. at Bath Coll. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1902; M.A., 1904; served with Middlesex Yeomanry in S. African war, 1900 (Queen's medal and three clasps); Egyptian exploration fund with Prof. Flinders Petrie, 1904 and 1905; asst. res., Northern Nigeria, 14th July, 1906; 3rd cls. res., 1st Apr., 1912.
PORTAL, E.—Asst. govt. med. officer, Seychelles, July, 1892; govt. med. officer, Mauritius (Rodrigues), 1901; asst. med. supt., Barkly, and lunatic asylums, 1st July, 1905.

PORTULLI-CARBONE, ALFONSO, M.D. (Malta Univ., 1883).—L.M. (Rot. Hosp., Dub.), 1890; police physician for Zurrico, Safi, Crendi, and act. pol. phys. for Micalibba and Chirop, and sanitary inspr. for the 6th country dist., Aug., 1884; dist. med. offr. for Valetta, Sept., 1885; is a mem. of the Br. Med. Assoc. (Mediterranean branch), 1888; fell. Br. Gynaecol. Soc., and mem. Intern. Med. Cong., Berlin, 1890, and Rome, 1894 (obst. sec.).

PORTER, WILLIAM THOMAS.—B. 1877; ed. at Univ. schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge B.A. (law), 1901; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1901; joined N. E. circuit, and pract. at York, Leeds, Bradford and West Riding of Yorks sessions; mag. E. Africa Prot., 10th May, 1907; ag. adminstr.-gen., July to Nov., 1907; town mag., Mombasa, Aug., 1907, to Sept., 1908; 1st cls. mag. for provinces of Kisumu and Naivasha, Sept., 1908, to Feb., 1909, town mag., Nairobi, 23rd Feb., 1909; prea., dist. ct., Cyprus, 26th Oct., 1911, to Mar., 1916; ag. puisme judge, Cyprus, May, 1915, to Mar., 1916, and several previous occasions; puisme judge, G. Coast, 14th Dec., 1915.

POTT, P. A. H.—B. 1877; asst. transport offr., Gold Coast, Sept., 1901; dist. commr., Ashanti, Jan., 1907; ag. cantonment mag., Coomassie, Oct. to Dec., 1907, and Apr.-May, 1909; ag. pol. mag., Coomassie, Nov., 1913 to July, 1914, and from Jan., 1915; ag. prov. comsnr., S. Prov., Ashanti, May and June, 1909, Sept. and Oct., 1913; ditto, W. Prov., Ashanti, Dec., 1910 to June, 1911, and from June to Dec., 1912.

POTTER, HENRY YARLEY DIGHTON.—B. 1875; ed. at Edinburgh Acad.; 2nd lieut., Edinburgh City Artill., July, 1894; lieut., Feb., 1896; passed in "Artillery," July, 1896; asst. engr., with Messrs. Blyth and Westland, consulting engrns. to North British Rly. Co., 1895; asst. engr. to Mr. John Strain, Glasgow, 1896; asst. engr., Caledonian Rly., 1897; draftsman, dir. of wks. dept., Admiralty, 1898; asst. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 21st Nov., 1901; state engrn.'s asst., Perak, 14th Feb., 1902; asst. engrn., Larut and Matang, Perak, 1st Jan., 1905; exec. engrn., 3rd grade, 1st Jan., 1906; ag. exec. engrn., 2nd grade, Perak, June, 1906; ditto, Mar., 1909; ditto, Pahang, Jan., 1910; exec. engrn., grade II., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1913; seconded for mil. ser. by F.M.S. govt., 1914; lieut., R.G.A., 6th Jan., to 28th May, 1915; resumed duty in F.M.S., 11th Aug., 1915.

POTTER, THOMAS IRWIN.—Ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; ag. asst. clk., col. hosp., 18th Dec., 1882; clerical asst. Crown lands off., 10th Apr., 1883; clk., survey dept. (in addition to former post), 1st Dec., 1884; ch. clk., Crown lands off., 16th Aug., 1897; ch. clk. and asst. inspr. of schls., 1st Nov., 1898; warden, La Brea and Oropuche (owing to spec. knowledge of asphalt question); ch. offr. of customs, Brighton, La Brea, 1st Jan., 1899; harb.-master, Brighton harbour, La Brea, in addition to wardenship, 1st July, 1899; J.P. for county of St. Patrick; mem. of Royal society of arts, and dormant mem. Zoological society of Lond.; ag. sub. int., crown lands, 1st Sept. to 31st Oct., 1901, and from 10th Oct., 1905, to Jan., 1906; ag. harb.-mast., Colony, June to Dec., 1906; stip. justice, La Brea, and Oropuche in conjunction with wardenship, 5th June, 1907; ag. harb.-mast., Colony, May to Nov., 1908; ag. sub-intendant, Nov., 1908, to April, 1909; ag. adminstr.-gen., 28th Apr., 1909; ag. offl. recvr. and adminstr.-gen., 1st Feb. to 13th Dec., 1910; ag. regier.-gen., 1st Apr. to 30th June, 1911; regier.-gen. (provisionally), 1st July, 1911; confirmed, 16th Aug., 1912; mem. of Port-of-Spain town board, 10th June, 1913; nominated mem. of Port-of-Spain city council, 31st Oct., 1914.
POTTS, HENRY WILLIAM, F.L.S., F.C.S.—Prin. of Hawkesbury Agric. Coll., Richmond, N. S. Wales, since May, 1902.

POTTS, MOSES A.—Ed. S. Leone Gram. schl.; served in H.M.'s commissariat, S. Leone, June, 1853, till Jan., 1860; ag. 2nd clk., treas., July, 1865, to Jan., 1866; extra clk., Jan., 1866; house and land tax clk., Aug., 1869; 2nd clk., treas., after a compet. exam., Sept., 1870; census master, 1871; ch. clk. and cashier, 1877; ag. aud.-gcn., Dec., 1886, to Feb., 1887; charity comsnr., 1890.

POUGNET, EDOUARD DESBARRIERES, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E.—Pub. wks. dept., Mauritius, Jan., 1888; asst. water authority, Mch., 1898; inspr. of water wks., July, 1903; govt. survr., Mch., 1911; supt. of pub. wks. and survr. of roads, St. Kitts, Apl., 1914; in service of French govt., in France during the war.
POULLIER, REGINALD SYDNEY VERNON.—B. 1894; B.A., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ serv., local divn., Aug., 1919; attached to Galle Kach., Aug., 1919.

POUNTNEY, ARTHUR MEIK.—B. 1873; ed. Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., 1896; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, 1903; ag. prot. of Chinese, Perak, 1904; ag. offl. assignee, 1905; ag. prot. of Chinese, Selangor, 1906; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, 1909; supt. of census, F.M.S., 1910; ag. prot. of Chinese, Selangor, 1912; treas., S. Settmts., 1st Feb., 1914.

POWELL, HENRY.—Student, Royal Gardens, Kew, June, 1888, to Apr., 1890; curator, Botanic Gardens, St. Vincent, May, 1890, to Nov., 1903; asst. to dir. of agric., E. Africa Prot., 7th Nov., 1903; ch. of economic plants div., Apr., 1907.

POWELL, HENRY TREVOR.—Ed. Monmouth schl.; apptd. 3rd asst. in treasry., Brit. E. Africa Prot., 1st Dec., 1901; 2nd asst., 1st Apr., 1903; offl. mem., plague arbitn. bd., 1903; treas., Somaliland Prot., 28th Nov., 1903; supt. of post offices, Nov., 1906; G.A.S. medals and 2 clasps Somaliland, 1902-1904 and 1908-1910.
POWELL ROBERT ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E., assoc. Roy. Coll. of Science, Dub.—Dist. engrn., P.W.D., Ceylon; ag. 2nd financial and office asst., Nov., 1892; dist. engrn., Oct., 1893; prov. engrn., Feb., 1914.

POWER, EDMUND LE POER.—B. 1872; asst. dist. comsnr., Somaliland Prot., 1901; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1903; ag. viceconsul, Zeyla, Oct., 1903; dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 1912; ag. prov. comsnr., E.A.P., 1915; Somaliland medals, 1902-1904, 1908-1910.
POWERS, HON. CHARLES—Justice of the high ct. of Australia, 1913.

POYNTON, HON. ALEXANDER.—Formerly M.H.A., S. Australia; mem. of house of representatives, Commonwealth of Australia; treas., C. of A., 14th Nov., 1916; ag. min. for the navy and min. in charge of shipping and shipbuilding Apl., 1918.

PRAIN, LT.-COL. SIR DAVID, KT. BACH. (1912); C.M.G. (1912); C.I.E. (1906); M.A., M.B., LL.D., F.R.S.E., F.L.S., F.R.S.—B. 1857; ed. Aberdeen gram. schl. and Univ. of Aberdeen and Edinburgh; demonstrator of anatomy, coll. of surgeons, Edin., 1882-3, and univ. of Aberdeen, 1883-1884; entd. Indian med. serv., 1884; curator, Calcutta herbarium, 1887-1888; prof. of botany, med. coll., Calcutta, 1896-1905; director, botanical survey of India, and supt. of royal botanic garden, Calcutta, 1898-1901; trustee of the Indian Museum, 1898-1904; sec. bd. of scientific advice for India, 1903-1904; mem. of coun. of various scientific societies; director of royal botanic gardens, Kew, since 1905.

PRANKERD, HORACE ARTHUR.—B. 1883; ed. at Ipswich and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (scholar, 2nd class Tripos), B.A., 1905; asst. master, Loretto, to July, 1907; then 2½ years at Lausanne and Freiburg-in-Baden Univa.; asst. sec., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1910; asst. res., 1911.

PRASAD, MURALI.—B. 1888; Cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1912; attached to Galle Kachcheri, Jan., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Point Pedro, Sep., 1913; addtl. office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1914; addtl. dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Aug., 1915; office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Dec., 1915; pol. mag., Chilaw, Apr., 1917; ditto, Panadure, Apl., 1919.

PRATT, CHARLES E. J. BLYTH.—Ed. Trinity Coll.; 2nd lieut., 4th batt. Sherwood Foresters, Derbyshire regt., Jan., 1887; lieut., 4th batt. Prince of Wales North Stafford regt., May, 1888; instructor of musketry, Jan., 1890; lieut., Falmouth div. submarine miners, R.E., Apr., 1893; asst. inspr. Sierra Leone frontier pol., Aug., 1896; inspr., Apr., 1896; J.P. for S. Leone.

PRATT, EDWARD.—Ed. at Shrewsbury; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1898; ag. asst. dist. offr., Raub, May, 1899; ag. collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1901; passed cadet, May, 1902; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Jan., 1903; ag. asst. dist. offr., Tanjong Malim, Sept., 1903; ag. dist. treas., Batu Gajah, Jan., 1904; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Nov., 1904; ditto, Tanjong Malim, Dec., 1904; ditto, Kuala Lipis, Aug., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Oct., 1908; ag. mag., Taiping, Oct., 1910; ag. regis., supreme ct., Kinta, May, 1911; ag. revenue auditor, Perak, 23rd Oct., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Krian, 7th July, 1913; offr. of cls. III, S. Sttlmts., 21st Dec., 1914.

PRATT, F. G., A.M.I.C.E.; asst. engrn., Lagos govt. rly., Mar., 1907; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Uganda, Sept., 1908; ditto, P.W.D., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1911; represented N. Nigeria at 3rd International Road Congress held in London in June, 1913; dir. of pub. wks., Nyasaland, 1917.

PRENDERGAST, HON. SIR JAMES, Kt. Bach. (1881).—B. 1828; is grad. of Univ. of Camb.; called to bar (Mid. Temp.), 1857; admitted a barrister of the sup. ct. in N. Zealand, Dec., 1862; atty.-gen., 1865; ch. just. of N.Z., 1875-1899.

PRENDERGAST, HON. JAMES EMILE PIERRE.—Ed. at Seminary of Quebec, and Laval Univ., Quebec; B.A., 1878; LL.B., 1881; returned for La Verendrye, Manitoba, Aug., 1885; again Dec., 1886; on formation of cabinet by Hon. Thomas Greenway, Jan., 1888, was given portfolio of prov. sec.; re-elected in said constituency; elected for Woodlands, 1888; judge of sup. ct., N.W.T., 1902; ditto, Saskatchewan, 1907; puisne judge, court of King's Bench of Manitoba, 1910.
PRESCOTT, ARTHUR STANLEY.—Appd. after compet. exam., supplementary clk., supreme ct., Gibraltar, 1895; 3rd cls. clk., 1899; asst. regis. and deputy clk. of arraigns, 1911.

PRETTY, ERIC ERNEST FALK.—B. 1891; ed. Harrow, and Demy of Magdalen Coll., Oxford; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1914; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1916; ag. collr. of land revenue, Johore Bahru, Sept., 1917; 2nd lieut. and asst. adjt., Johore Vol. Rifle, May, 1918.

PREVOST, EDWARD WILLIAM DE TOISSIER.—B. 1882; served in S. Africa as 2nd lieut., 3rd Royal Lancaster regt., 1901-2 (Queen's medal with two clasps); wing offr. of constab., and supt. of civil pol., N. N. Borneo, 1904-1911; passed higher standard exam. in Malay language; passed course of instruction at R.I.C. depot, Dublin; asst. comsnr. of pol., Gold Coast, Apr., 1912; passed lower standard exam. in Fanti language; comsnr. of pol., Jan., 1915; dep. inspr.-gen. of pol., Apr., 1917.

PRICE, G. F.—Dist. accnt., S. African govt. rlys. (construction), Natal, 1904-1907; accnt., Baro-Kano rly., N. Nigeria, Feb. to July, 1908; accnt., P.W.D., 1st Aug., 1908; asst. treas., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1910; ag. chief asst. treas., Aug. to Nov., 1912.
PRICE, HERBERT HENRY.—2nd clk. to asst. comsnr., Northern Div., Bech. Prot., 1910; 2nd clk., res. comsnr.'s office, 1st May, 1912; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1910.

PRICE, WALTER CROMWELL, B.A.—Ed. Rathmines Sch., Dublin, and at Dublin Univ. and Sch., of Engineering, Queen's Coll., Galway; asst. to James Price, A.M.I.C.E.; engaged on Galway harbour works, 1880; Lock Erne drainage, 1882; Irish light rlys., 1883-1884; Bandon water wks., 1883-1886; dist. eugnr., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1887; in charge of irrigtn. wks., 1888-1890; ag. prov. engrn., North-Central Prov., 1890; ditto, Uva Prov., 1902; ditto, Northern prov., 1902; prov. engrn., North-Western Prov., 1904; ditto, Central Prov., 1907; mem. of mun. coun., Kandy, and also of san. bd., Kandy, 1910; prov. engrn., W. Prov., Mar., 1914; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Jan., 1915; ditto, N. Prov., Oct., 1916.

PRICHARD, HERBERT WILLIAM.—B. 1873; ed. at Keble Coll., Oxford (open class. scholar, 1892); called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Jan., 1902; practised in London, 1902-1910; stip. mag., Trinidad, 19th Aug., 1910; 1st puisne judge, Mauritius, 19th Feb., 1915; 1st puisne judge, 20th Oct., 1916; ag. procureur and advoc.-gen., 30th Oct., 1916, to 2nd July, 1917; chrmn. of comsn. on Royal College, 1916-1917; chrmn., sugar sales comttee., 1st Sept., 1918 to 31st Aug., 1919.
PRICHARD, WILLIAM OWEN.—Med. offr., E. Africa Prot., 15th Feb., 1904; senr. med. offr., 1913.

PRINCE, EDWARD ERNEST, F.R.S.C., &c.—B. 1858; ed. Modern Schl., Leeds, and St. Andrews, Camb., and Edin. Univ.; apptd. demonstr., zool., Edin. Univ., 1880; naturalist, Scotch fishery bd. laboratory, St. Ands.; prof. of zool. and comp. anat., Royal Infirmary Med. Coll., Glasgow, 1890; fishery expert, Irish Deep Sea Survey, 1893; vice-pres. Internat. Fisheries Congress, Washington, Paris, and St. Petersburg, 1903-06-09; vice-pres., biol. sect., Brit. Assoc., 1909; ditto, Royal Soc. of Canada, 1908; mem. of internat. relations coun., American fish soc., 1910; Canad. mem. of internat. fish congress, Rome, 1911; life mem. of British Science Guild; mem. of internat. fisheries comsn.; repres. Canada, under Fisheries Treaty, 1908; chrmn. of many fishery comsns.: lobster, 1898; Pacific salmon, 1907; Bay of Fundy Shad, 1908; Manitoba fisheries, 1909; Alberta and Sask. fisheries, 1910-1911; chrmn. of biol. bd. of Canada; comsnr. of fisheries for Canada, apptd. 1892.
RING, ROBERT DARLOW.—Puisne judge, N. S. Wales, since 1902.

PRINSEPE, HENRY CHARLES.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; went to W. Australia, 1866; apptd. J.P. in 1867; ag. clk. and draftsman in lands dept., 1874; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to admstr., Nov., 1884, to June, 1885; ch. draftsman, lands dept., 1890; ch.clk.,1892; under-sec. for mines,1894; ch. prot. of aborigines, May, 1898.

PRIOR, A. C. VINCENT.—B. 1881; ed. King's Coll., Cambridge; M.A., LL.B.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1905; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, 5th Jan., 1915; ag. ch. just., Jan. to June, 1915; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., 8th Apr., 1918.

PRIOR, WM. BURGESS.—Mem. provisional N. Borneo Assoc.; res. E. Coast dist. Br. N. Borneo, Aug., 1881; prot. of coolies, E. Coast, 1884; mem. E. Coast coun., 1883; Br. consul. agt., 1880; res. and mag., Gold Field dist., Feb., 1887.
PRITCHARD, MAJOR C. G.—1st comsn. R.A., 23rd Nov., 1891; promoted capt., 4th Dec., 1899; adj., Hong Kong volunteers, Jan. to July, 1901; comdt. and adj. (local major), 2nd Apr., 1902.

PRITCHARD, STANLEY ARCHIBALD MARKHAM, C.M.G. (1918).—Served in Cape Mtd. Rifles, 1894-98; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mtd. Police, 1898-1901; private sec., to comsnr. for native affairs, Aug., 1901; ch. inspr., native affairs dept., Nov., 1901; pass comsnr. and ch. inspr., native affairs dept., July, 1908; asst. dir. of native labour, July, 1908; dir., Dec., 1908; major, U.D.F., political services in Prot. of S.W. Africa, 1915; colonel (staff-off.), South African Native Labour Contingent, 1916-18.

PRITCHETT, CECIL JOHN SUCKLING, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civil serv., Dec., 1912; attached to Jaffna Kachcheri, Jan., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Trincomalee, July, 1913; ag. asst. settlmt. offr., Nov., 1913; office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Nov., 1914; asst. collr. of customs and landing surrv., Jaffna, Nov., 1914; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Dec., 1915; office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Feb., 1916; pol. mag., Avissawella, Oct., 1916; ditto, Panadure, Dec., 1917; ditto, Chilaw, Apr., 1918; ag. distl. Galle, Sept., 1919.

PROBYN, SIR LESLIE, K.C.M.G. (1909); C.M.G. (1903).—Ed. at Charterhouse, and in France and Germany, called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1884; joint author of "The Jurisdiction and Practice of the Mayor's Court," and other law books; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, 1893; atty.-gen. of Grenada, May, 1896; also ag. col. sec. in 1897-8; ag. admstr. on several occasions; gov. secretary, S. Nigeria, 12th Apr., 1901; gov., Sierra Leone, 4th July, 1904; gov., Barbados, 5th Nov., 1910; assumed govt., 13th Feb., 1911; gov., Jamaica, 31st May, 1918; assumed govt., 11th June, 1918.
PROPSTING, HON. W. B., C.M.G. (1918).—M.L.C., Tasmania; atty.-gen. and min. for rly., Apr., 1916.

PROUSE, ARTHUR DUNCAN.—B. 1863; M.I.C.E.; asst. engrnr., harbour wks., Colombo, Ceylon, Feb., 1896; on spec. duty, Zanzibar, May to Aug., 1909; res. engrnr., Colombo harbour wks., Feb., 1913; harbour engrnr., Feb., 1913.

PROUT, W. T., C.M.G. (1905), M.B., C.M. (Edin.).—Asst. poor law med. offr., Mauritius, 1885; surg. supt., immigrant ship "Dundee," 1887; asst. col. surg., Gold Coast, 1888; dist. comnrr., Gold Coast, 1890; col. surg., Gambia, 1893; ag. chief surg., Gambia, 1893; med. offr. in charge of base hosp. during campaign against Foday Sila; col. surg., S. Leone, 1895, title altered to prin. med. offr., 1901; retired, 1906; fellow of roy. med. soc., Edin., and correspndg. mem. of the epidemiological soc. of Lond.; lect., schl. of trop. med., Univ. of Liverpool; med. adviser to C.O.; mem. of advisory, med. and sanitary comttee. for Tropical Africa, C.O.; author of article "Yaws" in "Diseases of Warm Climates" (Davidson); Fil. Volouloides (Archives de Parasitologie, May, 1901); "Filariasis in S. Leone" (Brit. Med. Journal, 1902); and other papers.

PRYCE, HOWARD LLOYD, C.M.G. (1911).—Trav. comsrr., Gambia Prot., Oct., 1896; attached to Gambia field force, 1901 (medal and clasp); ag. ch. mag., Gambia, July to Oct., 1903; 1st cls. trav. comsrr., Jan., 1904.

PTOLEMY, WM. JOHN, I.S.O., J.P.—B. 1850; ed. at Smithville gram. schl., Ontario; served with G.W. Rly. Co. of Canada, 1873-1875; C.P.R. Dominion govt. telegraph construction, 1875; mercantile life, 1876-1882; entd. govt. serv. as acctnt., treasy. dept., Manitoba, 1883 to 1885; municipal comsrr., 1886; dep. prov. treaser., since 1886.

PUDSEY, FAWCETT.—B. 1882; Assoc. M.I.M.E.; Assoc. M.I.C.E.; ed. Hymer's Coll., Hull, and Repton Schl.; apprentice with Messrs. Rose, Downs and Thompson, Ltd., Hull, 1900-1905; engineering asst., dock engrnr.'s staff, N.E. Rly., 1905-1911; engaged on surrey work, Catalonia, Spain, for the Pearson Engineering Corporation, New York, 1911; in charge of opening of the Hessle quarry and crushing plant of the British Portland Cement Manufacturers, Ltd., 1912-1914; asst. dir. of pub. wks., B. Guiana, 1914; ag. D.P.W., Feb., 1916.
PUGH, J.—Med. offr., E.A.F., Jan., 1910.

PUGHE, S. R.—B. 1877; apptd., after compet. exam., elk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1897; promoted, with gratuity, Aug., 1904; confir. elk., Dec., 1907; minor staff clerk, 1911; staff elk., 1st grade, 1st Mar., 1916.

PUGSLEY, HON. WILLIAM, K.C., D.C.L.—B. 1850; educ. schls., Sussex, N.E., Univ. of New Brunswick, B.A. (1868), and Univ. of Lond., B.C.L., D.C.L.; called to the bar, N.B., 1872; reporter of the supreme ct., 1873-83; Q.C. by Dom. govt., 1891; elec. to legis., N.B., 1885; speaker, 1887; solr.-gen., 1890; resigned, 1892; re-entrd. legis., 1899; attorney-gen., 1900; premier, 1907; sworn of the priv.-council for Canada and min. of pub. wks., 1907; re-elec. to H. of C., Sept., 1911; resig. portfolio on defeat of Laurier govt., Oct., 1911; lieut.-gov. of New Brunswick, 1918.

PURCELL, SIR GILBERT KENHELM TREFFRY, KT. BACH. (1916).—Ed. Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1888; M.A. 1893; called to bar, Linc.'s Inn, Nov., 1890; mem. of western circ., also practised at cent. crim. ct.; apptd. asst. to atty.-gen. Leeward Islas. (St. Kitts), Mar., 1898; official mem. of exec. and legis. couns. of the presidency of St. Christopher and Nevis; ag. atty.-gen. Leewards, Aug.-Oct., 1900; apptd. ag. 1st puisne judge, June, 1901; puisne judge, G. Comst., 11th June, 1901; chief justice, Sierra Leone, 1911.

PURDON, ANDREW.—B. 1875; ed. at Ebury and Conway cols., N. Wales; cadet, H.M.S. "Conway"; sub-lieut., R.N.R., Apr., 1905; lieut., 1908; qualified at Whale Island and H.M.S. "Vernon"; asst. marine supt., N. Nigeria Marine, 11th Sept., 1909; whilst on leave took special course of instruction in hydrographical survey at R.N. coll., Greenwich, 1911.

PURVES, JAMES MCLENNAN.—Govt. forester, Nyasa land, May, 1900; ag. head, forestry and botanic dept., Jan., 1906 to Feb., 1907, and Oct., 1907 to Feb., 1909; ch. forest offr., Dec., 1910; ag. dir. of agric., Aug., 1911 to May, 1912, May to Dec., 1914, and Sept., 1918 to Dec., 1919; served agst. rebels in Shire Highlands, 1915 (medal and clasp); chmn., food production and collection comttee, 1918-19.

QUARTEY, ROBERT EMANUEL.—B. 1863; outdoor offr., customs, Accra, 1881; 4th clk., customs and treasy., 1883; 2nd clk., audit office, 1885; 1st elk., 1887; audit clk. to local auditor, Jan., 1889; has several times acted as local auditor; transf. to post and tel. dept., 1902; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., 1903; in charge of post and tel. dept., 1903; sent to Axim on spec. P.O. duty, 1903; on spec. duty at Pram Prain, 1904; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., 1905 and 1906; dist. postmtr., Jan., 1907; taking spec. course of instr. at G.P.O., London, 1907.

QUICK, HON. SIR JOHN, KT. BACH. (1901); LL.D.—B. 1852; elec. to first H. of R., Commonwealth of Australia, 1901; re-elec. in 1903 and 1906; postmtr.-gen., C. of A., June, 1909; retired from politics, 1913.

RADCLIFFE, JOHN.—B. 1879; ed. Denstone and Royal Schl., Armagh; lieut., I.Y., S. African war, 1900-1901 (Queen's medal with 5 clasps); lieut. 5th, Batt., Leinster Regt., 1903; D.S.C., N. Nigeria, 1906; senr. transport offr., 1908; ag. chief transport offr., Aug., 1909, to Feb., 1910; ag. cantonment mag., Zungera, Jan., 1911 to July, 1912.

RADFORD, WILLIAM JOHN.—Charing Cross hosp.; M.R.C.S. (England), 1887; L.R.C.P. (London), 1890; F.R.G.S., 1900; hon. mem., St. John's ambulance soc., 1896; med. offr., E. Africa Prot., 1st June, 1898; sen. med. offr., 1910; chief sanitary offr., Apr., 1913.

RAE, JAMES STANLEY.—B. 1881; articled and conf. clk. to Sir W. R. Davies, Kt., when atty.-gen., Bahamas; ditto to Sir Anton Bertram, Kt., when atty.-gen., Bahamas; called to the bar, Bahamas, Apr., 1904; admitted as a student, Middle Temple, 1910 (attending terms on vacations); clk. to legis. coun., Bahamas, 1901-13; ag. coroner for the Is. of New Providence from 1904, confirmed in 1911; J.P., 1910; ag. atty.-gen. and advoc.-gen. admiralty advoc. and temp. mem. of the exec. coun., Jan., 1910; ag. stip. and circ. mag., 1910-11, and on other occasions; ag. pros. couns., for crown on several occasions; dist. commnr., Belize, B. Honduras, from 1914; offl. rec. in bankruptcy, and offl. admistr. and J.P. for the col., 1914; regis.-gen., Nov., 1915, in conjunction with duties as dist. commnr.; dir. of P.O.G. fund, 1916; custodian of enemy property, Sept., 1916.

RAINIER, GEORGE DIRK.—Temp. clk., mag's. off., Prince Albert, Cape Colony, July, 1881; clk., Oudtshorn, Apr., 1882; auditor of expenditure acts., C.S.O., Feb., 1886; ditto, atty.-gen's. off., 1889; auditor and acctnt. of rev., June, 1891; asst. mag., Malmesbury, Feb., 1892; asst. mag., Somerset West, 1896; ag. civ. commnr. and res. mag., for numerous districts; civ. commnr. and res. mag., Barkly West, Aug., 1900; and Middelburg, 1904; taxing master and asst. regir., asp. ct., Cape Town, 1906; also high sheriff for Cape Colony, 1908; insptg. mag. and inspr. of prisons, Apr., 1912; under sec. for justice, Union of S. Africa, July, 1912; ag. mem., public service commn., Sept., 1917.
RAINSFORD, R. F.—Asst. supt. of police, E.A.P., Apr., 1914.

RALPHS, EDWIN, F.R.G.S., F.C.S., F.E.I.S., M.R. San. Inst.—B. 1872; ed. St. Mark's Coll., Chelsea; 1st cls. in Queen's scholarship, in final teachers' exams. (education dept.), and in archbishops' divinity exam.; honours in science, South Kens.; asst. master (junior) Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1898; senior grade, 1901; Normal mast., 1906; ag. second master, Apr., 1903, to Mar., 1904, and Apr., 1908 to Apr., 1909; 2nd mast., 8th Apr., 1909; ag. head mast. and mem. of advisory commte., Tech. Inst., 5th July to 5th Nov., 1909, and from 11th May, 1910, to 25th Oct., 1911; ag. mast., pol. schl., Hong Kong, May, 1902, to Mar., 1904; hon. examnr. in chemistry to Hong Kong Coll. of Med., 1901, 1902 and 1903; dir., Hong Kong Technical Inst., from 1907; also mem. of exam. commte. for "Teachers' Classes," Tech. Inst., 1909-1911; J.P., 1910; inspr. of English schls., 1913; asst. commnr., St. John Ambulance Brigade, Hong Kong and China dist., 1915; seconded as British Red Cross commnr. in Siberia, Oct., 1918 to Feb., 1919; War off. commn. as (temp.) hon. capt., Jan., 1919; ag. dir. of educn., Oct., 1918, and Aug. to Dec., 1919.
RALSTON, JAMES, M.A., Glasgow Univ.—B. 1890; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1914; lecturer in mathematics, technical inst.; on active serv., 1918-19.

RAM, GEORGE EDWARD.—Lieut. 4th batt., Prince of Wales's, North Staffordshire Regt.; served in S. Africa, Oct., 1902, to July, 1903 (medal, with 3 clasps); ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to gov. of Newfoundland, 9th Sept., 1903.

RAMANATHAN, PONNAMBALAM, C.M.G. (1889), K.C.—B. 1861; ed. at the Acad., Colombo, and Prea. Coll., Madras; barr.-at-law. Inn. Tem.; advoc., sup. ct., Ceylon, 1873; mem. of the legis. coun., 1879; mem. of the law commn. apptd. to report upon certain codes dealing with the laws of Ceylon, 1879; served also on the commn. apptd. at his instance to report upon the Thoroughfares Ordinance, 1881; obtained the introduction of P.O. savings bank in Ceylon, 1881-83; was one of the select commte. of the legis. coun. apptd. to report upon retrenching the public expenditure of the island, 1882-83; a mem. of the coun. of legal educn., 1884; mem. of the Ceylon commn. of the Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; mem. of the Cent. Irrig. Bd., 1888; and of the select commte. to report upon the incidence of the grain taxes, 1889; mem. of the commn. to inquire into the extension of the rly. to the N. parts of the island, 1890; solr.-gen. of Ceylon, 1892; ag. atty.-gen., 1894; ret., 1906; elected M.L.C., Ceylon, 1911; re-elected, 1917.
RAMSBOTTOM, HON. A. E. W.—B. 1860; formerly col. treas., O.R.C.; prov. adminstr., O.F.S., Union of S. Africa, 1910.

RANEY, HON. W. E.—B. 1861; was schl. teacher for some years; also engaged in newspaper wk.; grad. in law at Osgoode Hall and took up practice in Toronto, 1892; has taken an active part in temperance and moral reform movements; atty.-gen. in U.F.O. govt., Nov., 1919.

RANFURLY, 5TH EARL OF (SIR UCHTER JOHN MARK KNOX, G.C.M.G.), Viscount Northland and Baron Welles of Dungannon, co. Tyrone, Ireland, Baron Ranfurly of Rampforlie, co. Renfrew, United Kingdom.—Born 1856; succeeded his brother 1875; ed. Trinity Coll., Cambridge; J.P., D.L. for co. Tyrone; Knight of Justice of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; lord-in-waiting to the Queen, 1895-7; governor of New Zealand, 1897-1904.

RANKINE, RICHARD SIMS DONKIN.—B. 1875; clk. treasury, Fiji, Feb., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir O'Connell O'Brien, Sept., 1897, to 1901; acted clk. to ex. coun. Sept., 1898, to Mar., 1900; apptd. acctnt. nativ. taxes, treasury, 1901; priv. sec. to Sir Henry Jackson, July, 1902; priv. sec. to Sir E. im Thurn, 10th Oct., 1904; conf. sec., 1st Jan., 1905; sec. to consul-gen. for W. Pacific; clk. of exec. coun.; ag. sec., W. Pacific high commn., June, 1905 to June, 1906, and from Nov., 1907, to 31st Dec., 1908; asst. col. sec. and asst. revcr.-gen., 1st Jan., 1909; revcr.-gen. and commnr. of stamps, pres. of marine bd., mem. excm. and legis. couns., 1910; ag. col. sec., Nov., 1913 to Nov., 1914; gov'r's dep., 5th to 14th June, 1914; commnr. of currency, Dec., 1914; ag. agt.-gen. of immigr. (conjoint), Mar., 1915; ag. col. sec., Aug., 1915 to May, 1916; gov'r's deputy, Aug., Oct., Dec., 1915; ag. col. sec., 18th Sept., 1917, to 6th Jan., 1918, and from 16th June to 9th Oct., 1918.
RANSOM, HERBERT CHARLES.—B. 1881; entd. crown agt.'s off., May, 1901; asst. cashier, Mar., 1914; ag. head of apptnts. dept. and asst. sec., Jan., 1917; head of miscellaneous dept., Apr., 1919.

RASON, HON. SIR CORINTHWAITE HECTOR, KT. BACH (1909).—B. 1859; ed. at Brighton, Eastbourne and Reading; M.L.C. for Swan, W. Aust., 1889; M.L.A. for S. Murchison, 1897-1901; ditto for Guildford since 1901; min. of works, 1901; min. of works and rlys., 1902-4; prem. of W. Aust., 1905; pres. of Royal comn. on mining, 1898, and of Royal comn. on immigr., 1905; agt.-gen. in London for W. Aust., 1906-1909.

RAVEN, ROBERT McPHERSON.—B. 1872; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; asst. engrnr., Admiralty, 1895; dist. engrnr., Gold Coast rly. construction, 1898; dist. engrnr., Nigerian rly., 1904; chief engrnr., E. African mil. rlys., German E. Africa (occupied territory), 1917; temporary gen. man., 1918-1919; mem. of Nigerian Land Contingent, Aug., 1914 to Dec., 1917; major, Rly. Corps, E. African Exped. Force, Feb., 1918.
RAYNE, H. A.—Asst. dist. comntr., E. Africa Prot., Deco., 1914.

READ, SIR HERBERT JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1918), C.B. (1914), C.M.G. (1907).—B. 1863; M.A., Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 1st class math. mods. and 1st class math. finals; clk., higher div., W.O., Feb., 1887; 2nd class clk., C.O., Jan., 1889; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Sept., 1896; priv. sec. to Mr. (afterwards Sir E.) Wingfield, Feb., 1898; 1st cls. clk., Oct., 1898; principal clk., 1st Apr., 1905; British delegate at African liquor confce. at Brussels, 1899; one of the Brit. delegates on boundy. comntr. at Berlin for the settm. of Anglo-German frontier in E. Africa, 1906; one of the Brit. plenipotentiaries at African liquor confce. at Brussels, 1906; mem. of hon. man. comttee. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908; chmn. of deptmnl. comttee. to enquire into W. African med. staff, 1909; visited E. Africa, 1911-12; asst. under-sec. of state, 10th March, 1916; regiar. of the Order of Saint Michael and Saint George.

REAKES, CHARLES JOHN, M.R.C.V.S., Eng., Doctor of Vet. Science, Univ. of Melbourne.—B. 1865; apptd. vet. to New Zealand govt., 1897; ch. vet., 1908; lieut.-col., N.Z. vet. corps, 1908; colonel, 1915; dir. of vet. services and remounts, N.Z. defence dept., 1908; dir., live stock div., agric. dept., 1909; dir.-gen. of agric., 1918.

REAL, HON. PATRICK.—B. 1847; called to the bar, Queensland, 1874; frequently acted as dist. ct. judge, and for some years was Crown procer in cent. dist.; mem. of Royal comn. on establishment of a Queensland Univ., 1891; judge of sup. ct., Queensland, 1890.

REDINGTON, DUDLEY CECIL.—B. 1883; ed. Clongowes Coll., Ireland; Natal police, 1903-11; Zanzibar police, 25th March, 1911; supt., 15th Mar., 1912; asst. comdt., 1st Feb., 1914; ag. comdt. of police and ag. gov. of prisons, 7th Oct., 1915 to 31st Nov., 1916.

REDLICH, VIVIAN PERCIVAL.—B. 1881; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Ceylon, Jan., 1908; ditto, Avissawella, June, 1910; apptd. to cls. V. of civ. serv., local divn., Jan., 1913; ag. itinerating pol. mag., W. Prov., Feb., 1913; addtnl. pol. mag., Kurunegala, June, 1916; ag. pol. mag., ditto, Nov., 1918; apptd. to cls. IV. of civ. serv., local divn., Dec., 1918; ag. dist. judge, Kegalla, Oct., 1919.

REED, HAYTHORNE.—B. 1873; M.A., Cantab.; ed. Bath Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; Western Circuit; lieut., S. African Constabulary, 1902-1908; 2nd mag., Zanzibar, Dec., 1908; 1st mag., 8th Dec., 1910; ag. asst. judge, June-Dec., 1910, Mar.-Aug., 1915, Apr.-Nov., 1916, June, 1917, to Feb., 1918, and from Mar. to Dec., 1919; ag. chief judge, 16th Dec., 1919; author of "A Commentary on the Moneylenders' Act, 1909," and "A Commentary on the Police Offences Ordinance, 1902, of the Orange River Colony."
REEP, ALFRED MILLS.—B. 1896; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil serv., and assigned to C.O., Dec., 1914.

REES, LEWIS CHAS. MORRIS PARKER.—B. 1876; ed. Kingston Coll., Yeovil and Merchant Venturers Tech. Coll., Bristol; art. pupil to Messrs. Foster and La Trobe, architects' and surveyors, Bristol, 1892; asst. to borough engrnr., Ilington, 1897; ch. asst. borough engrnr., Margate, 1900; prin. land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1902; also sec., squatters bd., 1905; lieut., Hong Kong vol. corps., 1909.

REEVES, FREDERIC WILLIAM.—B. 1876; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados, Christ's Coll., Camb., and Wren's Inst.; Barbados Schl., 1895; math. scholar, Christ's Coll., 1896; B.A. (senr. optime), 1898; M.A., 1906; asst.-inspr. of schools, Grenada, 1903; unofficial mem. of advisory comttee. of bd. of dir. of secondary educn., July, 1906; ag. inspr. of schls., Grenada, Dec., 1906, to Jan., 1907; ditto, St. Vincent, May to July, 1907; ag. ch. clk., audit office, Grenada, Dec., 1907, to Jan., 1908; ag. inspr. of schls., Grenada, Jan.-Feb., 1908; headmaster, gram. schl. and inspr. of schls., St. Vincent, Aug., 1908; mem., bd. of educn., Sept., 1908.

REEVES, HON. WILLIAM PEMBER.—Born Canterbury, N.Z., 1857; ed. in Colony; prov. scholarship holder, 1868 and 1872; N.Z. scholarships in classics and English, 1874; Somes scholar, Christ's Coll., Canterbury; first elected mem. of H. of R., 1887; joined Ballance Min., Jan., 1891, holding the portfolios of educn., just. and labour; contd. in off. in Seddon Min. to Jan., 1896; agt.-gen. for N.Z. in London, 1896; represented N.Z. at Internat. Commer. Congress at Philadelphia, 1899; mem. of Pacific Cable Comtce., 1899; high comsnr. for N.Z. in London, June, 1906; resigned, 1908, on apptmt. as director of London schl. of economics; mem. of senate, London Univ.; author of the "Long White Cloud," "State Experiments in Australia and New Zealand," and other publications relating to N.Z.

REFALO, MICHAEL ANGELO, C.B.E. (1918), B.A., LL.D.—B. 1876; Prof. of Commercial Law and History of Legislation, Malta Univ., 1908; asst. crown advocate, Malta, 1910; crown advocate, 1915; offl. mem. exec. coun. and of coun. of govt., 1915.
REFORD, JOHN HOPE, B.A., M.D., B. Ch., B.A.O. (R.U.I.), L.M. (Dub.), D.T.M. (Liverpool).—Civ. surg., South African Field Force, 1901-2; med. offr., Uganda Prot., 1st Aug., 1907.
REID, ALEXANDER, I.S.O. (1904).—Ent. the govt. ser., Tasmania, 1858; under treasurer, 1895.

REID, ARTHUR WILLIAM.—Ed. at Leicester and Stafford; asst. store dept., Mersey rly., Liverpool, Jan., 1886; chief in 1889; asst. loco. supt. (stores), Uganda rly., 1897; son. asst., 1899; chief storekeeper, 1903.
REID, CAMPBELL BENNETT, M.B. (Edin.).—B. 1863; entd. med. ser., Trinidad, 1st Jan., 1889; dist. med. offr., 15th July, 1914.

REID, IRVINE KEMP.—M.B., C.M. (1884), M.D. (1887), D.P.H. (1st class honours in practical hygiene) (1891), Abdn. Univ.; J.P. (1904); mem. of Roy. Med. Soc., Edin.; mem. of Soc. of Med. Offrs. of Health; house surg. and sec., W. Norfolk and Lynn hosp., King's Lynn, 1886; surg., res. med. staff, Col. hospital, Georgetown, Br. Guiana, 1887-89; govt. med. offr., Is. of Wakenaam, Rio Essequibo, 1889; med. offr., penal stltmt., Massaruni; and res. surg. pub. hosp., Bartica, 1892; med. offr., Mara dist., 1893; ditto, Peter's Hall dist., and mem. med. bd., 1894; med. offr., Cotton Tree dist., 1895; med. offr., Plaisance dist., 1914; mem. med. bd., 1915.

REID, HON. JOHN DOWSLEY.—B. 1859; ed. pub. schl., Prescott, Queen's Univ., and Royal Coll. Physicians and Surgeons, Kingston, Ontario; physician; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1891, 1896, 1900, 1904, 1908, 1911 and 1917; mem. of privy coun. for Canada, and min. of customs in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; min. of rlyws. and canals, National govt., Oct., 1917.

REID, JOHN KIDSTON.—B. 1863; ed. High Schl., Stirling, and in Germany and France; entered pub. serv., Tasmania, 8th Jan., 1884; clk.-asst., legis. coun., 1887; clk. of H. of A., 1896; clk.-asst. of the fed. coun. of Australasia for several sessions prior to federation; sec. to Tasmanian representatives in the fed. convention which framed the C. of A. Constitution Act; mag., Tasmania, since 1903; an elder of the Presbyterian Church of Australia in St. Andrew's Church, Hobart.

REID, JOHN MCINTOSH.—B. 1873; entered Impl. customs service by compet. exam., 1893; asst. collr. of customs, S. Leone, 1906; consolidated and revised S. Leone customs regns.; comptroller of customs and registr. of shipping, B. Guiana, 1909; prepared code of customs regns. for B. Guiana; B. Guiana delegate at reciprocity confce., Ottawa, 1912, and at steamship confce. at Trinidad, 1913; served in S. African war with 2nd Batt., The Black Watch and 1st Batt., Scottish Horse, 1900-1902 (Queen's and King's medals and five clasps); adjutant, 4th (Res.)
